title: Hacker News Guidelines
text:
Hacker News Guidelines
Hacker News Guidelines
What to Submit
On-Topic: Anything that good hackers would find interesting. That includes
more than hacking and startups. If you had to reduce it to a sentence,
the answer might be: anything that gratifies one's intellectual
curiosity.
Off-Topic: Most stories about politics, or crime, or sports, or
celebrities, unless they're evidence of some interesting new
phenomenon. If they'd cover it on TV news, it's probably off-topic.
In Submissions
Please don't do things to make titles stand out, like using uppercase

title: Hacker News Guidelines
text:
In Submissions
Please don't do things to make titles stand out, like using uppercase
or exclamation points, or saying how great an article is.
Please submit the original source.
If a post reports on
something found on another site, submit the latter.
Please don't use HN primarily for promotion. It's ok to post
your own stuff part of the time, but the primary use of the site
should be for curiosity.
If the title includes the name of the site, please take it out,
because the site name will be displayed after the link.
If the title contains a gratuitous number or number + adjective,

title: Hacker News Guidelines
text:
If the title contains a gratuitous number or number + adjective,
we'd appreciate it if you'd crop it.
E.g. translate "10 Ways
To Do X" to "How To Do X," and "14 Amazing Ys" to "Ys."
Exception:
when the number is meaningful, e.g. "The 5 Platonic Solids."
Otherwise please use the original title, unless it
is misleading or linkbait; don't editorialize.
If you submit a video or pdf, please warn us by appending
[video] or [pdf] to the title.
Please don't post on HN to ask or tell us something. Send it
to hn@ycombinator.com.
Please don't delete and repost. Deletion is for things

title: Hacker News Guidelines
text:
to hn@ycombinator.com.
Please don't delete and repost. Deletion is for things
that shouldn't have been submitted in the first place.
Don't solicit upvotes, comments, or submissions. Users should vote and
comment when they run across something they personally find
interesting—not for promotion.
In Comments
Be kind. Don't be snarky. Converse curiously; don't cross-examine.
Edit out swipes.
Comments should get more thoughtful and substantive,
not less, as a topic gets more divisive.
When disagreeing, please reply to the argument instead of calling

title: Hacker News Guidelines
text:
When disagreeing, please reply to the argument instead of calling
names. "That is idiotic; 1 + 1 is 2, not 3" can be shortened to "1 + 1
is 2, not 3."
Don't be curmudgeonly. Thoughtful criticism is fine, but please don't
be rigidly or generically negative.
Don't post generated text or AI-edited text. HN is for conversation
between humans.
Please don't fulminate. Please don't sneer, including at the rest of
the community.
Please respond to the strongest plausible interpretation of what
someone says, not a weaker one that's easier to criticize. Assume good
faith.

title: Hacker News Guidelines
text:
someone says, not a weaker one that's easier to criticize. Assume good
faith.
Eschew flamebait. Avoid generic tangents. Omit internet tropes.
Please don't post shallow dismissals, especially of other people's
work. A good critical comment teaches us something.
Please don't use Hacker News for political or ideological battle. It
tramples curiosity.
Please don't comment on whether someone read an article. "Did you
even read the article? It mentions that" can be shortened to "The
article mentions that".
Please don't pick the most provocative thing in an article or post to

title: Hacker News Guidelines
text:
article mentions that".
Please don't pick the most provocative thing in an article or post to
complain about in the thread. Find something interesting to respond to
instead.
Throwaway accounts are ok for sensitive information, but please don't
create accounts routinely. HN is a community—users should have an
identity that others can relate to.
Please don't use uppercase for emphasis. Instead, put *asterisks*
around it and it will get italicized. More formatting
info
here .
Please don't post insinuations about astroturfing, shilling,

title: Hacker News Guidelines
text:
info
here .
Please don't post insinuations about astroturfing, shilling,
brigading, foreign agents, and the like. It degrades discussion
and is usually mistaken. If you're worried about abuse, email
hn@ycombinator.com and we'll look at the data.
If a story is spam or off-topic, flag it. Don't feed egregious
comments by replying;
flag
them
instead. If you flag, please don't also comment that you did.
Please don't complain about tangential annoyances—e.g.
article or website formats, name collisions, or back-button breakage.
They're too common to be interesting.

title: Hacker News Guidelines
text:
They're too common to be interesting.
Please don't comment about the voting on comments. It never does any
good, and it makes boring reading.
Please don't post comments saying that HN is turning into Reddit.
It's
a
semi - noob
illusion ,
as
old
as
the
hills .

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
Hacker News FAQ
Hacker News FAQ
Are there rules about submissions and comments?
https://news.ycombinator.com/newsguidelines.html
How are stories ranked?
The basic algorithm divides points by a power of the time since a
story was submitted. Comments in threads are ranked the same way.
Other factors affecting rank include user flags, anti-abuse software,
software which demotes overheated discussions, account or site weighting,
and moderator action.
How is a user's karma calculated?
Roughly, the number of upvotes on their posts minus the

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
How is a user's karma calculated?
Roughly, the number of upvotes on their posts minus the
number of downvotes. These don't match up exactly. Some votes are
dropped by anti-abuse software.
Do posts by users with more karma rank higher?
No.
Why don't I see down arrows?
There are no down arrows on stories. They appear on comments after
users reach a certain karma threshold, but never on direct replies.
Why do I see an orange/red asterisk?
It just means it's your own post.
Why are my links grey instead of black like other links?

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
It just means it's your own post.
Why are my links grey instead of black like other links?
It means you've visited it before. If you click on a black link it will turn grey.
What kind of formatting can you use in comments?
http://news.ycombinator.com/formatdoc
How do I submit a question?
Use the submit link in the top bar, and leave the url field blank.
How do I submit a poll?
http://news.ycombinator.com/newpoll
How do I make a link in a text submission?
You can't. This is to prevent people from submitting a link
with their comments in a privileged position at the top of the

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
with their comments in a privileged position at the top of the
page. If you want to submit a link with comments, just submit
the link, then add a regular comment.
What are Ask HN and Show HN?
Ask HN
lists questions and other text submissions.
Show HN
is for sharing your personal work and has
special
rules .
Why hasn't my submission appeared on Ask HN or Show HN?
All Ask HNs appear on
newest
and
asknew , and all Show HNs on
newest
and
shownew , but there
is a small points threshold before a post makes it to
ask
or
show .
What do green usernames mean?
Green indicates a new account.

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
ask
or
show .
What do green usernames mean?
Green indicates a new account.
Why are some comments faded?
Faded text means that a comment has been downvoted. You can read the comment
in normal text by clicking on its timestamp to go to its page.
What does [flagged] mean?
Users flagged the post as breaking the
guidelines
or otherwise not belonging on HN.
Moderators sometimes also add [flagged] (though not usually on
submissions), and sometimes turn flags off when they are unfair.
How do I flag a comment?
Click on its timestamp to go to its page, then click the 'flag' link

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
How do I flag a comment?
Click on its timestamp to go to its page, then click the 'flag' link
at the top. There's a small karma threshold before flag links appear.
What does [dead] mean?
The post was killed by software, user flags, or moderators.
Dead posts aren't displayed by default, but you can see them all
by turning on 'showdead' in your profile.
If you see a [dead] post that shouldn't be dead, you can vouch for it.
Click on its timestamp to go to its page, then click 'vouch' at the
top. When enough users do this, the post is restored. There's a small

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
top. When enough users do this, the post is restored. There's a small
karma threshold before vouch links appear.
What does [deleted] mean?
The author deleted the post, or asked us to. Unlike
dead
posts, these remain deleted even when showdead
is turned on.
What do 'hide' links do?
They remove a submission from your personal view. Others still see it.
Are reposts ok?
If a story has not had significant attention in the last year or so,
a small number of reposts is ok. Otherwise we bury reposts as duplicates.
Please don't delete and repost the same story. Deletion is for things

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
Please don't delete and repost the same story. Deletion is for things
that shouldn't have been submitted in the first place.
Are paywalls ok?
It's ok to post stories from sites with paywalls that have workarounds.
In comments, it's ok to ask how to read an article and to help other
users do so. But please don't post complaints about paywalls. Those
are
off topic . More
here .
Can I ask people to upvote my submission?
No. Users should vote for a story because they personally find it
intellectually interesting, not because someone has content to

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
intellectually interesting, not because someone has content to
promote. We penalize or ban submissions, accounts, and sites that
break this rule, so please don't.
Can I ask people to comment on my submission?
No, for the same reason. It's also not in your interest: HN
readers are sensitive to this and will detect it, flag it,
and use unkind words like 'spam'.
Can I post a job ad?
Please don't post job ads as submissions to HN.
A regular "Who Is Hiring?" thread appears on the first weekday of each
month (or Jan 2). Most job ads are welcome there. Only an account called
whoishiring
is allowed to

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
month (or Jan 2). Most job ads are welcome there. Only an account called
whoishiring
is allowed to
submit the thread itself. This prevents a race to post it first.
Another kind of job ad is reserved for YC-funded startups. These
appear on the front page, but are not stories: they have no vote arrows,
points, or comments. They begin partway down and fall steadily.
Only one is on the front page at a time. The rest are listed
at
jobs .
What's the relationship between YC and HN?
Y Combinator
owns and funds HN. The
HN team is
editorially
independent .
HN gives three features to YC:
job ads (see

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
HN team is
editorially
independent .
HN gives three features to YC:
job ads (see
above ) and startup launches
get placed on the front page, and YC founder names
are displayed to other YC alumni in orange.
Are negative stories about YC suppressed on HN?
We moderate
less, not more , when YC or a YC startup is the topic.
The good will of the community is worth more than any story.
Why can't I post a comment to a thread?
Threads are closed to new comments after two weeks, or if the
submission has been killed by software, moderators, or user flags.

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
submission has been killed by software, moderators, or user flags.
Why is A ranked below B even though A has more points
and is newer?
You can't derive rank from votes and time alone. See "How are stories ranked?" above.
In my profile, what is delay?
It gives you time to edit your comments before they appear to others.
Set it to the number of minutes you'd like. The maximum is 10.
In my profile, what is noprocrast?
It's a way to help you prevent yourself from spending too much time on
HN. If you turn it on you'll only be allowed to visit the site for

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
HN. If you turn it on you'll only be allowed to visit the site for
maxvisit minutes at a time, with gaps of minaway minutes in between.
The defaults are 20 and 180, which would let you view the site for 20
minutes at a time, and then not allow you back in for 3 hours.
How do I reset my password?
If you have an email address in your profile, you can do that
here . If
you haven't, email hn@ycombinator.com for help.
Can I change my username?
Yes. Email hn@ycombinator.com and we'll help.
Can I delete my account?
We try not to delete entire account histories because that would gut

title: Hacker News FAQ
text:
Can I delete my account?
We try not to delete entire account histories because that would gut
the threads the account had participated in. However, we care about
protecting individual users and take care of privacy requests every
day, so if we can help, please email hn@ycombinator.com. We don't want
anyone to get in trouble from anything they posted to HN. More
here .
My IP address seems to be banned. How can I unban it?
If you request many pages quickly, your IP address might get
banned.
This
self-serve
unbanning procedure
works most of the time. If not, email hn@ycombinator.com.

title: Hacker News Security
text:
Hacker News Security
Hacker News Security
If you find a security hole, please let us know at
security@ycombinator.com . We try to respond (with fixes!) as soon as possible, and really appreciate the help.
Thanks to the following people who have discovered and responsibly disclosed security holes in Hacker News:
2025-07-08:
Benjamin Flesch
Poll options could be used for XSS.
2023-01-02:
Carter Sande ,
Mark Slater ,
James Darpinian
Submission titles were no longer being HTML-escaped in some places.
2022-09-04: Dimitris Triantafyllidis

title: Hacker News Security
text:
2022-09-04: Dimitris Triantafyllidis
User karma could be increased by exploiting an upvote/unvote bug.
2021-07-04:
RyotaK
URL tricks could display the wrong domain for some websites.
2021-06-07:
Atamyrat Hezretgulyyev
A CSRF logout was still possible in some cases.
2021-02-14: Michael Brooks
Set the SameSite cookie attribute for better CSRF protection.
2017-04-30:
Michael Flaxman
The minor version of bcrypt used for passwords was susceptible to a collision in some cases.
2017-04-14: Blake Rand
Links in comments were vulnerable to an IDN homograph attack.
2017-03-15: Blake Rand

title: Hacker News Security
text:
Links in comments were vulnerable to an IDN homograph attack.
2017-03-15: Blake Rand
The right-to-left override character could be used to obscure link text in comments.
2017-03-01:
Jaikishan Tulswani
Logged-in users could bypass 'old password' form field.
2016-02-17: Eric Tjossem
Logout and login were vulnerable to CSRF.
2016-01-13:
Mert Taşçi
The 'forgot password' link was vulnerable to reflected XSS.
2015-09-07: Sandeep Singh
An open redirect was possible by passing a URL with a mixed-case protocol as the
goto
parameter.
2015-09-04:
Manish Bhattacharya

title: Hacker News Security
text:
goto
parameter.
2015-09-04:
Manish Bhattacharya
The site name display for stories was vulnerable to an
IDN homograph attack.
2015-08-27: Chris Marlow
Revisions to HN's markup caused an HTML injection regression.
2015-06-24:
Stephen Sclafani
A form handling bug led to a XSS vulnerability using
HTTP parameter pollution .
2015-03-02: Max Bond
Information leaked during /r processing allowed an attacker to discover valid profile edit links and the user for which they were valid.
goto
parameters functioned as open redirects.
2014-11-01: Ovidiu Toader

title: Hacker News Security
text:
goto
parameters functioned as open redirects.
2014-11-01: Ovidiu Toader
In rare cases some users' profiles (including email addresses and password hashes) were mistakenly published to the Firebase API.
More
here .
2014-10-27: San Tran
Some pages displaying forms were vulnerable to reflected XSS when provided malformed query string arguments.
2014-05-01:
Jonathan Rudenberg
Some YC internal pages were vulnerable to persistent XSS.
2012-08-01:
Louis Lang
Redirects were vulnerable to HTTP response splitting via the
whence
argument.
Persistent XSS could be achieved via the
X-Forwarded-For
header.

title: Hacker News Security
text:
whence
argument.
Persistent XSS could be achieved via the
X-Forwarded-For
header.
2012-07-20: Michael Borohovski
Incorrect handling of unauthenticated requests meant anyone could change rsvp status for Demo Day.
2010-01-12:
Zain Memon
Someone creating a new account could sometimes take an existing username.
2009-06-03:
Daniel Fox Franke
The state of the PRNG used to generate cookies could be determined from observed outputs. This allowed an attacker to fairly easily determine valid user cookies and compromise accounts.
More
here .
Missing From This List?

title: Hacker News Security
text:
More
here .
Missing From This List?
If you reported a vulnerability to us and don't see your name, please shoot us an email and we'll happily add you. We crawled through tons of emails trying to find all reports but inevitably missed some.

title: Show HN Guidelines
text:
Show HN Guidelines
Show HN
Show HN is for something you've made that other people can play with.
HN users can try it out, give you feedback, and ask questions in the
thread.
On topic: things people can run on their computers or hold in their
hands. For hardware, you can post a video or detailed article. For
books, a sample chapter is ok.
Off topic: blog posts, sign-up pages, newsletters, lists, and other
reading material. Those can't be tried out, so can't be Show HNs. Make
a regular submission instead.
The project should be non-trivial. Don't post quickly-generated

title: Show HN Guidelines
text:
a regular submission instead.
The project should be non-trivial. Don't post quickly-generated
one-offs; anybody can do that now. Share something that is deeply
personal and interesting to you. Explain how and why.
The project must be something you've worked on personally and which
you're around to discuss. See these
tips
about how to present your work.
A Show HN needn't be complicated or look slick. The community is
comfortable with work that's at an early stage.
Please make it easy for users to try your thing out, ideally without

title: Show HN Guidelines
text:
Please make it easy for users to try your thing out, ideally without
barriers such as signups or emails. You'll get more feedback that way.
If your work isn't ready for users to try out, please don't do
a Show HN. Once it's ready, come back and do it then. Don't post
landing pages or fundraisers.
To post,
submit
a story whose title begins with
"Show HN".
Every Show HN appears on
shownew . Once it clears
a small points threshold, it will appear on the
show
page in the top bar.
New features and upgrades ("Foo 1.3.1 is out") generally aren't substantive

title: Show HN Guidelines
text:
page in the top bar.
New features and upgrades ("Foo 1.3.1 is out") generally aren't substantive
enough to be Show HNs. A major overhaul is probably ok.
Please don't ask friends to upvote or comment. That's
not ok
on HN.
In Comments
Be respectful. Anyone sharing work is making a contribution, however modest.
Ask questions out of curiosity. Don't cross-examine.
Instead of "you're doing it wrong", suggest alternatives. When someone
is learning, help them learn more.
When something isn't good, you needn't pretend that it is, but don't be
gratuitously negative.

title: Formatting Options | Hacker News
text:
Formatting Options | Hacker News Formatting Options Blank lines separate paragraphs.
Text surrounded by asterisks is italicized.
To get a literal asterisk, use \* or **.
Text after a blank line that is indented by two or more spaces is formatted as code.
Urls become links, except in the text field of a submission.
If your url gets linked incorrectly, put it in <angle brackets> and it should work.

Mumbai BMW Crash Lucknow Coaching Institute Fire Follow Us

We will do everything to secure their future," he said. The exam was conducted in 13 languages, including Hindi and English. text:
More Rapid Read
Like this summary? Share Your Feedback Shinde was accompanied by six rebel MPs on stage on Monday.
Shiv Sena UBT Crisis Highlights:
Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray is facing a fresh political challenge as six of his party’s
nine Lok Sabha MPs formally joined
the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena on Monday. Shinde, accompanied by all rebel MPs, addressed a press conference today where the big announcement was made.

title: Shiv Sena UBT Crisis Highlights: 6 Rebel MPs Formally Switch Camps, Shinde Calls Them 'Dhurandhars' - News18
text: Speaking about the six MPs who joined his camp, Eknath Shinde said, “We spoke to all the 6 MPs on the last couple of days. They are actually ‘real stars’.” Highlighting their commitment to public service, he added, “They are not here for their personal benefit, they are here to work for people of their constituencies.” Referring to Omraje Nimbalkar, Shinde said, “It is sad that court case didn’t work in his favour.”

title: Shiv Sena UBT Crisis Highlights: 6 Rebel MPs Formally Switch Camps, Shinde Calls Them 'Dhurandhars' - News18
text: The rebel MPs’ crossover has given the rebel bloc the two-thirds strength required under the anti-defection law. Meanwhile, Shiv Sena (UBT) held a meeting with its party MLAs and MLCs at Shivalay in Mumbai’s Nariman Point on the first day of the Maharashtra Legislature’s monsoon session. The meeting was being seen as an attempt by Thackeray to prevent further defections and reassure party legislators.

title: Shiv Sena UBT Crisis Highlights: 6 Rebel MPs Formally Switch Camps, Shinde Calls Them 'Dhurandhars' - News18
text:
Earlier, two dissident MPs, Nagesh Patil Ashtikar and Omprakash Raje Nimbalkar, had already confirmed their decision to switch sides, while leaders from the ruling Mahayuti alliance on Sunday had hinted that more political developments could follow.

text: Like this summary? Share Your Feedback Saamana Targets Amit Shah, BJP and Eknath Shinde in Explosive Editorial. Amid speculation over a possible split in the Shiv Sena (UBT) parliamentary party under the banner of ‘Operation Tiger’, the party mouthpiece Saamana on Monday launched a sharp attack on Union Home Minister Amit Shah, the BJP and Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde. The editorial comes at a time when Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray is facing a major political challenge, with six of the party’s nine Lok Sabha MPs reportedly preparing to join the Eknath Shinde-led

title: Uddhav Sena Questions BJP's 'Real Shiv Sena' Claim Amid Split Within: 'How Can Amit Shah Decide?' | India News - News18

title: Uddhav Sena Questions BJP's 'Real Shiv Sena' Claim Amid Split Within: 'How Can Amit Shah Decide?' | India News - News18
text: Amit Shah’s ‘real Shiv Sena’ remark In its editorial, Saamana criticised Amit Shah for referring to the Eknath Shinde-led faction as the “real Shiv Sena". The Uddhav Thackeray camp claimed the remark reflected Shah’s political anxiety and frustration. The editorial questioned how Shah could decide which faction was the legitimate Shiv Sena when the dispute over the party’s identity and symbol remains pending before the Supreme Court. It argued that Shah’s comments amounted to pre-judging a matter that is still under judicial consideration. The editorial also made broader political claims,

title: Uddhav Sena Questions BJP's 'Real Shiv Sena' Claim Amid Split Within: 'How Can Amit Shah Decide?' | India News - News18
text: matter that is still under judicial consideration. The editorial also made broader political claims, alleging that Shah appears to be positioning himself for a future bid for the Prime Minister’s post with the support of leaders such as Eknath Shinde. It further suggested that Prime Minister Narendra Modi should remain cautious about Shah’s political ambitions. Taking the attack further, the UBT faction alleged that Shah, with the help of leaders like Shinde, had sought to weaken the political challenge posed by Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis. However, all these remarks and

title: Uddhav Sena Questions BJP's 'Real Shiv Sena' Claim Amid Split Within: 'How Can Amit Shah Decide?' | India News - News18
text: challenge posed by Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis. However, all these remarks and allegations were made in the Shiv Sena (UBT) mouthpiece Saamana. Neither the BJP nor Amit Shah has issued any official response so far. Six Sens (UBT) MPs likely to switch camps The editorial appeared amid reports that six of Shiv Sena (UBT)’s nine Lok Sabha MPs are likely to formally join the Shinde-led faction on Monday. According to media reports, the MPs are expected to merge with the Shinde camp around 3 pm. If the move takes place, the group will attain the two-thirds strength required under

title: Uddhav Sena Questions BJP's 'Real Shiv Sena' Claim Amid Split Within: 'How Can Amit Shah Decide?' | India News - News18
text: around 3 pm. If the move takes place, the group will attain the two-thirds strength required under the anti-defection law, shielding the MPs from disqualification. Sources in the Shinde faction said photographs and videos of six rebel MPs meeting the Lok Sabha Speaker are likely to be released later in the day. The move is being projected as a sign that ‘Operation Tiger’ has reached its conclusion. Uddhav hits back at BJP Responding to Amit Shah’s remarks, Uddhav Thackeray asserted that the Shiv Sena founded by his father, Bal Thackeray, had spent three decades fighting the Congress but never

title: Uddhav Sena Questions BJP's 'Real Shiv Sena' Claim Amid Split Within: 'How Can Amit Shah Decide?' | India News - News18
text: Sena founded by his father, Bal Thackeray, had spent three decades fighting the Congress but never attempted to destroy or take over the party in the manner he alleged the BJP had done. “Shiv Sena, headed by me, is the only Shiv Sena," Thackeray said. Aaditya attacks rebel MPs Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Aaditya Thackeray also launched a strong attack on the rebel MPs. In a post, he accused them of abandoning the mandate on which they were elected and alleged that they had won with the support of the Maha Vikas Aghadi and INDIA bloc partners. Addressing the MPs, Aaditya said their actions showed

title: Uddhav Sena Questions BJP's 'Real Shiv Sena' Claim Amid Split Within: 'How Can Amit Shah Decide?' | India News - News18
text: the Maha Vikas Aghadi and INDIA bloc partners. Addressing the MPs, Aaditya said their actions showed that “their loyalty" and “reputation" were for sale. He said leaders from Shiv Sena (UBT), the Congress and the NCP had campaigned for them during the Lok Sabha elections and that voters in their constituencies had chosen INDIA bloc candidates over those of the NDA. He alleged that the MPs had deserted that mandate overnight due to greed. Uddhav convenes legislators’ meeting Amid the fast-moving political developments, Uddhav Thackeray has convened a key meeting of Shiv Sena (UBT) MLAs on the

title: Uddhav Sena Questions BJP's 'Real Shiv Sena' Claim Amid Split Within: 'How Can Amit Shah Decide?' | India News - News18
text: political developments, Uddhav Thackeray has convened a key meeting of Shiv Sena (UBT) MLAs on the opening day of the Maharashtra Legislature’s Monsoon Session. Watch more videos Share this Article WhatsApp facebook Twitter telegram copy link The meeting is scheduled for 2.30 pm at the party’s Shivalay office in Nariman Point, Mumbai. All party legislators have been directed to attend as the leadership assesses the fallout of the developments linked to ‘Operation Tiger’. text:

India retained rights over the eastern rivers---Ravi, Beas and Sutlej. (AI-Generated Image) Pakistan is at it again. The country’s defence minister

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: Khawaja Asif , in yet another attempt to needle India, has warned that Islamabad could resort to military action if it believes the country’s water security is under serious threat. The threat comes amid escalating tensions following New Delhi’s suspension of the Indus Waters Treaty. “The moment we feel that our national security and water are being threatened, we will go to war against India. Definitely," Asif said, while speaking to ARY News. Recommended Stories 'Will Go To War': Pakistan's Defence Minister Khawaja Asif Threatens India Over Indus Waters Treaty Khawaja Asif Slams Afghan

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: Defence Minister Khawaja Asif Threatens India Over Indus Waters Treaty Khawaja Asif Slams Afghan Taliban Over Inaction Against Militants Attacking Pakistan Pakistan's Moment In The Sun: From Bystander To Broker In The US-Iran Peace Deal Facing Indus Waters Treaty Fallout, Bilawal Bhutto Threatens India In Pakistan Parliament: 'We'll Retaliate' The statement has brought back attention to the

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text:
Indus Waters Treaty (IWT)
which, for more than six decades, was often described as one of the world’s most successful water-sharing agreements. It survived multiple India-Pakistan wars, military crises and prolonged diplomatic breakdowns. Even when bilateral relations collapsed, the treaty endured. ALSO READ |

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: Facing Indus Waters Treaty Fallout, Bilawal Bhutto Threatens India In Pakistan Parliament: ‘We’ll Retaliate’ However, all that changed after the April 2025 terror attack in Pahalgam, which killed 26 people. In its aftermath, India announced that it was placing the treaty “in abeyance" until Pakistan took credible and irreversible action against cross-border terrorism. The decision marked the most serious challenge to the treaty since it was signed in 1960. The move has triggered a wider debate: What was the treaty designed to achieve? Why does India now consider it outdated? And what could the

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: What was the treaty designed to achieve? Why does India now consider it outdated? And what could the consequences be for Pakistan? A Treaty Born Out Of Partition The origins of the Indus Waters Treaty lie in the chaos of Partition. When British India was divided in 1947, the six rivers of the Indus basin suddenly flowed across two newly created countries. While many of the rivers originated in India, large irrigation networks built during British rule were located in what became Pakistan. The problem surfaced almost immediately. In April 1948, India temporarily stopped water flows from some

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: problem surfaced almost immediately. In April 1948, India temporarily stopped water flows from some canals serving Pakistan, highlighting how vulnerable the new country was to upstream decisions. The dispute soon escalated into one of the first major India-Pakistan disagreements after Partition. ALSO READ |

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: India Launches Two Major Chenab Projects Amid Indus Treaty Freeze | Exclusive The negotiations stretched for years before the World Bank stepped in as a mediator. The eventual solution involved dividing the river system rather than sharing individual rivers. This became the foundation of the treaty signed by Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru and Pakistani President Ayub Khan in Karachi on September 19, 1960. How The Treaty Works The treaty divided the six rivers of the Indus basin into two groups. Pakistan received near-exclusive rights over the three western rivers—the Indus, Jhelum and

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: groups. Pakistan received near-exclusive rights over the three western rivers—the Indus, Jhelum and Chenab. India retained rights over the eastern rivers—Ravi, Beas and Sutlej. This allocation was unusually generous from an upstream perspective. Various official and academic assessments note that nearly 80 per cent of the basin’s waters were effectively assigned to Pakistan, while India received control over the eastern rivers. India was allowed limited use of the western rivers for domestic consumption, irrigation and run-of-the-river hydropower projects, but significant restrictions were

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: consumption, irrigation and run-of-the-river hydropower projects, but significant restrictions were placed on storage and diversion. The arrangement ensured predictable water flows to Pakistan’s vast agricultural economy, particularly in Punjab and Sindh, where millions of farmers depend on irrigation from the Indus system. Why The Treaty Was Considered Exceptional The treaty’s reputation rests largely on its durability. It survived the wars of 1965, 1971 and 1999, as well as decades of diplomatic hostility. Even after major terrorist attacks such as Mumbai in 2008 and Pulwama in 2019, neither

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: hostility. Even after major terrorist attacks such as Mumbai in 2008 and Pulwama in 2019, neither side formally abandoned the agreement. The treaty also created a permanent institutional mechanism through the Permanent Indus Commission, allowing technical discussions to continue even when political dialogue had broken down. Why India Began Seeking Changes India’s dissatisfaction with the treaty

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: began much before the Pahalgam attack. New Delhi has increasingly argued that the agreement was drafted under very different circumstances and does not adequately account for present-day realities such as climate change, changing water requirements, clean-energy needs and population growth. At the UN Human Rights Council this month, India described the treaty as “outdated" and criticised Pakistan for repeatedly obstructing permitted Indian hydropower projects through legal and arbitration processes. Indian officials have argued that Pakistan has used dispute-resolution provisions in ways that

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: Indian officials have argued that Pakistan has used dispute-resolution provisions in ways that delay infrastructure projects rather than resolve technical disagreements. New Delhi has also maintained that it has never fully utilised the rights available to it under the treaty. In January 2023, India formally notified Pakistan that it wanted modifications to the agreement. A second notice followed in 2024, signalling growing frustration with the treaty’s existing framework. How Pahalgam Changed The Equation The

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: April 2025 attack

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: transformed a long-running policy debate into a national-security issue. Following the attack, India announced that the treaty would be held in abeyance until Pakistan ended support for cross-border terrorism. The decision was part of a broader package of measures that linked bilateral engagement directly to security concerns. The government’s argument was straightforward: a treaty built on cooperation and goodwill cannot function normally if one side believes it is facing sustained terrorist threats originating from the other. What Does ‘In Abeyance’ Actually Mean? Contrary to some public

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: threats originating from the other. What Does ‘In Abeyance’ Actually Mean? Contrary to some public perceptions, India has not physically stopped the rivers from flowing into Pakistan. The geography of the basin makes an immediate cutoff impossible. Existing infrastructure does not allow India to suddenly divert or store the enormous volumes of water carried by the western rivers. Instead, the practical implications are more gradual. By suspending cooperation mechanisms, India is no longer treating treaty obligations as fully operational. Officials have suggested that India will move more

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: treating treaty obligations as fully operational. Officials have suggested that India will move more aggressively to maximise its rights over western-river waters through infrastructure development, storage projects and hydropower facilities. Why Pakistan Is Worried Pakistan’s concerns stem from its extraordinary dependence on the Indus basin. ALSO READ |

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: IWT Paused, Infra Fast-Tracked, Pakistan Irked: A Look At India’s Projects On Chenab Agriculture contributes significantly to the country’s economy and relies heavily on irrigation supplied by the Indus river system. Large parts of Pakistan’s food production, rural employment and water security are linked directly to these rivers. Experts frequently describe Pakistan as one of the world’s most water-stressed countries. Any reduction in flows, even gradual or seasonal, can have economic consequences. For Islamabad, therefore, the issue extends beyond diplomacy. It touches food security,

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: For Islamabad, therefore, the issue extends beyond diplomacy. It touches food security, electricity generation, irrigation planning and long-term economic stability. This explains why Pakistani officials have reacted strongly to India’s latest position, characterising it as a potential “water war" and warning about broader regional consequences. Can India Actually Stop The Water? Not in the short term. Most experts agree that India currently lacks the storage capacity needed to halt flows on a large scale. Building reservoirs, diversion structures and associated infrastructure would take years

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: scale. Building reservoirs, diversion structures and associated infrastructure would take years and require substantial investment. What India can do is progressively utilise a larger share of water that the treaty already permits it to use, while accelerating projects that had previously moved slowly. Over time, that could reduce the volume of water reaching Pakistan during certain periods and increase India’s leverage as the upstream state. Irrespective of the change in modalities, what is clear is that a water-sharing agreement once celebrated as untouchable has entered its most

title: The Indus Waters Treaty Survived Wars. Here's Why Khawaja Asif Says It May Become Cause For One | Explainers News - News18
text: what is clear is that a water-sharing agreement once celebrated as untouchable has entered its most consequential phase since 1960. text: 1 Tour, 3 Goals: Why Uddhav Thackeray’s Visit To Rebel MPs’ Turfs Matters Amid Sena Crisis According to sources, Shrikant Shinde played a central role in maintaining contact with MPs, keeping communication channels open and addressing concerns of leaders who were considering a shift. since the 2022 split. Last week,

Now, there is no faction. There is only one Shiv Sena." Watch more videos Share this Article WhatsApp facebook Twitter telegram copy link On the other hand, a resilient

title: Birthday Party Ends In Horror: BMW Crash On Mumbai-Delhi Expressway Kills Two, Body Parts Scattered | India News - News18
text: Like this summary? Share Your Feedback According to police, the incident occurred around 3 am. A birthday celebration turned tragic when a speeding BMW crashed into a divider and overturned several times on the under-construction Mumbai-Delhi Expressway near Badlapur in the early hours of Sunday, killing two youths and injuring their friend, who was driving the vehicle. According to police, the incident occurred around 3 am. The vehicle was heading towards Badlapur from Titwala when, near the Aeranjad area, the speeding car lost control and hit a roadside iron divider. The impact was so severe

title: Birthday Party Ends In Horror: BMW Crash On Mumbai-Delhi Expressway Kills Two, Body Parts Scattered | India News - News18
text: area, the speeding car lost control and hit a roadside iron divider. The impact was so severe that the car was thrown nearly 200 metres and was completely damaged. title: Birthday Party Ends In Horror: BMW Crash On Mumbai-Delhi Expressway Kills Two, Body Parts Scattered | India News - News18
text: Under Probe The deceased were identified as Yogesh Negi (26), a resident of Badlapur (West) who was celebrating his birthday, and Rebecca Jacob (24), a resident of Bandra. The BMW owner, identified only as Angad from Badlapur, sustained serious injuries and is currently undergoing treatment at Fortis Hospital in Kalyan. It is not yet clear whether the vehicle’s airbags deployed during the crash and contributed to his survival. To restrict vehicle entry into the under-construction expressway, cement barricades have been installed at multiple points. Police suspect that the BMW, while moving at

title: Birthday Party Ends In Horror: BMW Crash On Mumbai-Delhi Expressway Kills Two, Body Parts Scattered | India News - News18
text: barricades have been installed at multiple points. Police suspect that the BMW, while moving at high speed, may have tried to dodge one such concrete barrier, after which the driver lost control, and the vehicle crashed into the central divider, Times of India reported. The impact is believed to have caused the car to flip and come to a halt between two sections of the divider. text: Expressway near Badlapur has been completed, it has not yet been opened to the public as construction is still pending on other parts of the corridor. As many as 15 people were killed while several others sustained injuries after a major fire broke out at a commercial building in Lucknow on Monday afternoon. The fire incident prompted an extensive firefighting and rescue operation. According to the fire department, an alert about the blaze was received around 3 pm from a coaching centre situated on Usha Mehta Marg in the Aliganj police station area. Nearly 22 lakh students will be taking the NEET

title: CBSE 10th Second Board Result 2026 Live: CBSE Class 10 Improvement Result Date And Time Update - News18
text: To pass the CBSE Class 10 examination, students must secure at least 33% marks. Along with marks, the Board also awards grades based on performance. The board follows a 9-point grading system ranging from A1 to E, with A1 being the highest grade and E indicating failure.

title: CBSE 10th Second Board Result 2026 Live: CBSE Class 10 Improvement Result Date And Time Update - News18
text: As per the grading system of CBSE, the top one-eighth of successful candidates are awarded Grade A1, followed by Grade A2 for the next one-eighth. Grades B1 and B2 are assigned to the subsequent two one-eighth segments of passed students. Similarly, Grades C1, C2, D1, and D2 are allotted to the remaining one-eighth groups of successful candidates. Students who do not pass the examination are awarded Grade E.

title: CBSE 10th Second Board Result 2026 Live: CBSE Class 10 Improvement Result Date And Time Update - News18
text:
This year, nearly 6.7 lakh students registered for the second board examinations. Among them, around 5.25 lakh students appeared for improvement exams, while more than 85,000 candidates took the examination under the compartment category. Students who successfully clear the Class 10 examination will be eligible for admission to Class 11.

text:
+ 0 % 'Operation Tiger Successful': 6 Rebel UBT MPs Formally Cross Over To Shinde Sena
Published By
: Anoshito Banerjee , News18.com Last Updated: June 22, 2026, 19:17 IST Six Shiv Sena UBT MPs join Eknath Shinde Shiv Sena, Shinde calls it second phase of 2022 rebellion, touts Hindutva, welfare schemes and double engine sarkar reset Rapid Read Rapid Read Summarized by AI. +
More Rapid Read

title: 'Operation Tiger Successful': 6 Rebel UBT MPs Formally Cross Over To Shinde Sena | India News - News18
text: Like this summary? Share Your Feedback Maharashtra Deputy CM and Shiv Sena chief Eknath Shinde | File image: PTI Six Members of Parliament from Uddhav Thackeray’s Shiv Sena (UBT) formally joined Eknath Shinde’s Shiv Sena on Monday, with Shinde declaring the inductions complete and calling the development the second phase of the 2022 rebellion that ended Thackeray’s government in Maharashtra. Addressing a gathering with all six MPs standing alongside him, Shinde said: “Today, there are six tigers present here." He quipped that three of the six MPs share the first name Sanjay, calling it “triple

title: 'Operation Tiger Successful': 6 Rebel UBT MPs Formally Cross Over To Shinde Sena | India News - News18
text: present here." He quipped that three of the six MPs share the first name Sanjay, calling it “triple Sanjay power," and took an indirect swipe at Shiv Sena (UBT) spokesperson Sanjay Raut, saying: “When we have Sanjay here, no need to talk about any other Sanjay." Recommended Stories 4 Reasons Why Eknath Shinde Attacked Uddhav Thackeray With ‘Bow And Arrow’ Again ‘Those Who Want To Leave Can Go’: Uddhav Thackeray’s Message To Party MPs Amid ‘Op Tiger’ Buzz Uddhav Thackeray To Tour All Constituencies Of Rebel MPs, Review Ground Situation Uddhav Thackeray Offers To Resign As UBT Sena Chief, Calls

title: 'Operation Tiger Successful': 6 Rebel UBT MPs Formally Cross Over To Shinde Sena | India News - News18
text: Of Rebel MPs, Review Ground Situation Uddhav Thackeray Offers To Resign As UBT Sena Chief, Calls Rebel MPs 'Traitors' “We have hit a sixer now," Shinde said, drawing on the cricket metaphor to describe the six-MP induction. He said the original 2022 decision to break from the Thackeray faction was taken to protect Bal Thackeray’s Hindutva ideology, and that Monday’s event continued that effort. “Wherever there is the ideology of Shiv Sena, that is where Shiv Sena is," he said. Shinde said he had personally spoken to all six MPs over the preceding two days and described them as “real stars" who

title: 'Operation Tiger Successful': 6 Rebel UBT MPs Formally Cross Over To Shinde Sena | India News - News18
text: personally spoken to all six MPs over the preceding two days and described them as “real stars" who had joined not for personal gain but to serve their constituencies. On MP Nimbalkar, Shinde said it was “sad" that a court case had not gone in his favour. Defending the 2022 split, Shinde recalled predictions that none of the 40 MLAs who backed him would win re-election. “I had said if they don’t win, I will resign and go back to my village and become a farmer," he said. He said 60 were subsequently re-elected. How It Happened The six MPs are Sanjay Haribhau Jadhav, Sanjay Dina Patil, Sanjay

title: 'Operation Tiger Successful': 6 Rebel UBT MPs Formally Cross Over To Shinde Sena | India News - News18
text: re-elected. How It Happened The six MPs are Sanjay Haribhau Jadhav, Sanjay Dina Patil, Sanjay Uttamrao Deshmukh, Bhausaheb Rajaram Wakchaure, Nagesh Bapurao Patil Ashtikar, and Omprakash Bhupalsingh Nimbalkar. The Shiv Sena (UBT) had nine Lok Sabha MPs. The six needed to meet the two-thirds threshold for a valid merger. Their absence from a parliamentary party meeting called by the Thackeray camp in Delhi on June 17 first signalled the impending switch. MPs Arvind Sawant, Anil Desai, and Rajabhau Waje attended that meeting, along with Rajya Sabha MP Sanjay Raut. On Sunday, Nagesh Patil

title: 'Operation Tiger Successful': 6 Rebel UBT MPs Formally Cross Over To Shinde Sena | India News - News18
text: Rajabhau Waje attended that meeting, along with Rajya Sabha MP Sanjay Raut. On Sunday, Nagesh Patil Ashtikar and Omprakash Raje Nimbalkar publicly confirmed their decision to join the Shinde camp. The Shiv Sena (UBT) said it had begun disciplinary proceedings against the six rebel MPs. The Shinde camp’s effort to bring the MPs across had been called “Operation Tiger," a reference to the tiger symbol of the original undivided Shiv Sena founded by Bal Thackeray. Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis acknowledged the success of the operation and blamed Uddhav Thackeray for the crisis.

title: 'Operation Tiger Successful': 6 Rebel UBT MPs Formally Cross Over To Shinde Sena | India News - News18
text: Fadnavis acknowledged the success of the operation and blamed Uddhav Thackeray for the crisis. Thackeray, addressing party workers in Bhandup on Sunday, said he was not demoralised and would continue to lead what he described as the original Shiv Sena. text:
Chocolate lovers in Bengaluru have something exciting to look forward to this World Chocolate Day. Premium chocolate brand SMOOR is set to host SMOORLAND, a special three-day festival packed with chocolate-themed experiences and activities.
by
Priyanshi Shah
Updated: June 24, 2026 4:57 PM
Share

title: World Chocolate Day: SMOOR Brings India’s Largest Chocolate Playground To Bengaluru; Check Dates, Tickets | Curly Tales
text:
by
Priyanshi Shah
Updated: June 24, 2026 4:57 PM
Share
No matter whether you’re a kid, teenager, or adult, chocolate is everyone’s favourite. For me, it has always been a way to keep my childhood alive and make celebrations extra special. This World Chocolate Day, Bengaluru is set to get a unique chocolate-filled experience with SMOOR that goes far beyond simply enjoying desserts. Read on to know the details!
SMOOR To Turn Bengaluru Into A Chocolate Lover’s Paradise
The premium chocolate brand
SMOOR

title: World Chocolate Day: SMOOR Brings India’s Largest Chocolate Playground To Bengaluru; Check Dates, Tickets | Curly Tales
text:
SMOOR To Turn Bengaluru Into A Chocolate Lover’s Paradise
The premium chocolate brand
SMOOR
is all set to host SMOORLAND, India’s largest chocolate playground, in Bengaluru. The brand is organising a three-day
chocolate festival
at its newly launched Experience Centre in Bengaluru on July 4, 5, and 7. At this event, visitors can enjoy fun activities, live experiences, and behind-the-scenes chocolate-making sessions.

title: World Chocolate Day: SMOOR Brings India’s Largest Chocolate Playground To Bengaluru; Check Dates, Tickets | Curly Tales
text:
The venue, spread across nearly 68,000 sq. ft., offers visitors a chance to explore how chocolate is made, from bean to bar, through guided factory tours and interactive experiences. Visitors can attend chef-led workshops, sample different chocolates, complete interactive challenges and earn rewards throughout the event. Exclusive surprises and limited-edition experiences will also be available.
Also Read:  Why A Bengaluru Woman Initially “Hated” The City, Now Can’t Imagine Leaving It Behind
Make Your Own Chocolate Bar At SMOORLAND

title: World Chocolate Day: SMOOR Brings India’s Largest Chocolate Playground To Bengaluru; Check Dates, Tickets | Curly Tales
text:
Make Your Own Chocolate Bar At SMOORLAND
One of the most exciting parts of SMOORLAND is that you can create your own personalised chocolate bar with the help of SMOOR’s master chocolatiers. You can also put your taste buds to the test in blind chocolate-tasting challenges, hunt for hidden truffles in a giant treasure ball pit and take part in several fun games like Frosted in the Dark cupcake decorating challenge, Blind Cocoa Challenge, tarot sessions, family games, customised keepsakes and meet-and-greets with popular pop-culture characters.

title: World Chocolate Day: SMOOR Brings India’s Largest Chocolate Playground To Bengaluru; Check Dates, Tickets | Curly Tales
text:
A major highlight of the festival is the launch of the SMOORLAND Auction House. Guests can collect coins by participating in activities and use them to bid for premium chocolates, desserts, gift hampers and special experiences available only during the festival.
Expanding beyond the Experience Centre, on July 7, SMOOR will bring parts of the festival to
Kempegowda International Airport , Bengaluru. While passing through the airport, travellers can participate in activities such as Hot Chocolate Pong, Frosted in the Dark and the Blind Cocoa Challenge.
Where : SMOOR Experience Centre

title: World Chocolate Day: SMOOR Brings India’s Largest Chocolate Playground To Bengaluru; Check Dates, Tickets | Curly Tales
text:
Where : SMOOR Experience Centre
When : 11 AM to 1:30 PM and 2:30 PM to 5 PM
Cost : ₹1,599 above 13 years, ₹749 for 6 to 12 years, while entry is free for children below six years

Varanasi Ropeway: Reach Kashi Dham From Cantt Station In 16 Min; Check Fares & Discounts
The ropeway is all set to connect the four major stations like the Varanasi Cantt, Vidyapeeth, Rath Yatra and Godowlia Chowk.

title: Varanasi Ropeway: Reach Kashi Dham From Cantt Station In 16 Min; Check Fares & Discounts | Curly Tales
text:
by
Tanvi Shrivastava
Updated: June 24, 2026 5:59 PM
Share
Travelling to Kashi Vishwanath Temple is about to get much more convenient. Varanasi’s upcoming ropeway project, which is also India’s first urban public transport ropeway, is almost near its completion, and fares have already been finalised. Want to know more about it? Read on!
Varanasi Ropeway Nears Its Completion

title: Varanasi Ropeway: Reach Kashi Dham From Cantt Station In 16 Min; Check Fares & Discounts | Curly Tales
text:
Varanasi Ropeway Nears Its Completion
Once completed, travellers will be able to commute from Varanasi Cantt Station to Godowlia in just 15-16 minutes, greatly reducing travel time and aiming to ease traffic congestion in the city. As per a report by NDTV, the maximum fare for the entire route has been set at ₹50.

title: Varanasi Ropeway: Reach Kashi Dham From Cantt Station In 16 Min; Check Fares & Discounts | Curly Tales
text:
The ropeway is all set to connect the four major stations like the Varanasi Cantt, Vidyapeeth, Rath Yatra and Godowlia Chowk. According to the fare structure, people travelling the full stretch from Cantt to Godowlia will pay ₹50, while people opting for much shorter trips, such as Vidyapeeth to Rath Yatra, will pay as little as ₹20.
Also Read:  What Is Walking Pedestrian System In Ahmedabad? Check How It Helps Safe Road Crossing

title: Varanasi Ropeway: Reach Kashi Dham From Cantt Station In 16 Min; Check Fares & Discounts | Curly Tales
text:
Also Read:  What Is Walking Pedestrian System In Ahmedabad? Check How It Helps Safe Road Crossing
To benefit locals and frequent travellers, people who hold the Kashi Smart Pass will receive a 20% discount on fares. This means the journey from Cantt to Godowlia will cost ₹40 instead of ₹50. Not only this, but the monthly pass holders will be eligible for a 30% discount.

title: Varanasi Ropeway: Reach Kashi Dham From Cantt Station In 16 Min; Check Fares & Discounts | Curly Tales
text:
The ropeway stops will also offer passenger-friendly facilities. The facilities include the cloakroom at Varanasi Cantt station and will allow travellers to store luggage free of charge for up to two hours. After that, a fee of ₹50 per hour will apply for baggage weighing up to 15 kg.
Premium Gandola & Modern Amenities
The project will also feature premium gondola services that are customised for tourists and group bookings. While a private gondola ride will cost ₹2,000, bookings by companies or groups will be available at ₹1,200 per gondola.

title: Varanasi Ropeway: Reach Kashi Dham From Cantt Station In 16 Min; Check Fares & Discounts | Curly Tales
text:
All four stations have been furnished with modern facilities, including lifts, escalators, CCTV surveillance, fire safety systems and digital ticketing. Special requirements have also been made for senior citizens, women and differently abled passengers.
Once fully operational, the ropeway will enhance connectivity between Varanasi Cantt Railway Station, Godowlia, Dashashwamedh Ghat and Kashi Vishwanath Dham, providing a faster, cleaner and more efficient alternative to navigating the city’s congested roads.

title: Varanasi Ropeway: Reach Kashi Dham From Cantt Station In 16 Min; Check Fares & Discounts | Curly Tales

FAQs
Is Varanasi ropeway started or not?
The Varanasi (Kashi) Ropeway is nearing its completion but has not officially opened for public passenger operations yet.
What is the cost of ropeway in Varanasi?

title: Varanasi Ropeway: Reach Kashi Dham From Cantt Station In 16 Min; Check Fares & Discounts | Curly Tales
text:
What is the cost of ropeway in Varanasi?
The Varanasi Ropeway costs ₹10 to ₹50 for standard travel, depending on your route.
Is ropeway available in Banaras?
Yes! Varanasi is home to India’s first-ever urban public transport ropeway.

Why Lohagad Fort Is A Risky Monsoon Trek, And Precautions To Keep In Mind
Situated around 65 km from Pune and about 100 km from Mumbai, the 18th-century Lohgad Fort attracts thousands of trekkers.
by
Tanvi Shrivastava

title: Why Lohagad Fort Is A Risky Monsoon Trek, And Precautions To Keep In Mind | Curly Tales
text:
by
Tanvi Shrivastava
Updated: June 24, 2026 6:21 PM
Share
Monsoon season is here, and it surely turns the whole of Maharashtra state into a paradise. People often go trekking up the hills and to the fort to relish the season. One of the important sites is the Lohagad Fort, which transforms into one of Maharashtra’s most popular weekend escapes. But the trek is not as simple as it sounds; here are all the things you need to keep in mind before visiting the fort.
Lohgad Fort During The Monsoon Season
Situated around 65 km from
Pune

title: Why Lohagad Fort Is A Risky Monsoon Trek, And Precautions To Keep In Mind | Curly Tales
text:
Lohgad Fort During The Monsoon Season
Situated around 65 km from
Pune
and about 100 km from Mumbai, the 18th-century fort attracts thousands of trekkers to see the beauty of mist-covered hills, overflowing waterfalls and its iconic Vinchu Kata (Scorpion’s Tail) ridge. But while the rains make Lohagad absolutely beautiful, they also make it considerably more risky.

title: Why Lohagad Fort Is A Risky Monsoon Trek, And Precautions To Keep In Mind | Curly Tales
text:
The biggest challenge during the monsoon here in the fort is the slippery terrain. The stone steps that lead up to the fort become lined with moss and algae, making them very slick. Even skilled trekkers can lose balance, specifically during heavy rain. Another risky cause during the monsoon season is the strong winds and poor visibility, which is caused by dense fog and can also make certain sections risky.
Also Read:  This Hidden Gem In Gujarat Will Transport You To Europe With Its Colourful Bohra Havelis &Old-World Architecture

title: Why Lohagad Fort Is A Risky Monsoon Trek, And Precautions To Keep In Mind | Curly Tales
text:
In addition to this, another key factor that involves risk is overcrowding. Lohagad has become one of the most popular and easily accessible monsoon treks, all thanks to its proximity to Lonavala railway station. Thousands of visitors often gather at the fort during the weekends, creating delays on narrow pathways and raising the chances of accidents.
Things To Keep In Mind During The Trek
If you’re planning a trip to
Lohgad Fort

title: Why Lohagad Fort Is A Risky Monsoon Trek, And Precautions To Keep In Mind | Curly Tales
text:
Things To Keep In Mind During The Trek
If you’re planning a trip to
Lohgad Fort
this monsoon, then preparation is essential. It is said that wearing trekking shoes with a strong grip instead of sneakers or sandals is advisable. Avoid carrying umbrellas, as strong winds can make them difficult to manage, and instead choose a good-quality raincoat.
Lohagad is undoubtedly one of Maharashtra’s most beautiful rainy-season destinations. But it’s also a reminder that monsoon treks should be treated as outdoor adventures, not casual outings.

title: Why Lohagad Fort Is A Risky Monsoon Trek, And Precautions To Keep In Mind | Curly Tales

FAQs
Why is Lohagarh famous?
Lohagarh Fort ("Iron Fort") in Bharatpur, Rajasthan, is famous for being one of the only invincible castles in Indian history.
Is Lohagad Fort Trek difficult?

title: Why Lohagad Fort Is A Risky Monsoon Trek, And Precautions To Keep In Mind | Curly Tales
text:
Is Lohagad Fort Trek difficult?
No, the Lohagad Fort trek is generally considered easy to moderately easy.
How many stairs are there to Lohgad?
The Lohagad Fort has between 250 and 300 steps reaching up to its top.

Bangalore is hosting India's biggest craft chocolate festival to celebrate the journey of Indian cacao.
by
Shreya Rathod

title: Chocolate Lovers, India’s Biggest Craft Chocolate Festival Is Happening In Bangalore This Nov!
text:
by
Shreya Rathod
Updated: November 17, 2023 6:25 PM
Share
While chocolate has traditionally been associated with happiness, wealth, and good times, good handmade chocolate is made using a variety of scientific methods. Although the majority of us occasionally indulge ourselves in something chocolate-y, very few of us are aware of the natural process involved in producing chocolate and what goes into creating an artisanal chocolate bar. To celebrate the journey of Indian cacao at India’s biggest craft chocolate festival in Bangalore.

title: Chocolate Lovers, India’s Biggest Craft Chocolate Festival Is Happening In Bangalore This Nov!
text:
India’s Biggest Craft Chocolate Festival Is Happening In Bangalore
Credits: Press Release

title: Chocolate Lovers, India’s Biggest Craft Chocolate Festival Is Happening In Bangalore This Nov!
text:
India’s Biggest Craft Chocolate Festival Is Happening In Bangalore
Credits: Press Release
The “Indian Cacao & Craft Chocolate Festival” is the place to go if you want to sample some of the most amazing craft chocolate made in India and discover more about how cacao evolved from a common bean to a high-end treat. This is India’s first and largest craft chocolate festival, organised by the craft chocolate community. Over two days, some of the best chocolatiers, cacao growers, industry professionals, and aficionados will be together under one roof, alongside some of the best craft chocolate manufacturers.

title: Chocolate Lovers, India’s Biggest Craft Chocolate Festival Is Happening In Bangalore This Nov!
text: Patricia Cosma, a bean-to-bar chocolate expert and consultant from Romania, and Ketaki Churi, a chocolatier, maker, and product developer from Mumbai, are the minds behind the festival. Patricia, who began her adventure into the world of chocolate in 2016, feels that India has enormous potential when it comes to chocolate—possibly even more than Europe. She discovered the exceptional quality of cacao in India as she continued to visit cacao farms around South India and sample chocolates from around the globe.

title: Chocolate Lovers, India’s Biggest Craft Chocolate Festival Is Happening In Bangalore This Nov!
text:
She discovered that there is insufficient understanding of how to process high-quality cacao beans in India after researching the process of creating chocolate, post-harvesting methods, and cacao farming. The event, which aims to raise awareness of handmade chocolate and establish a community where enthusiasts can collaborate, will include the best of the best in the chocolate industry, including both individual and brand-name chocolate manufacturers.

Bengaluru has always grabbed attention. While some talk about its growing tech scene, others complain about the constant traffic. However, not many people talk about what it feels like to be completely new to the city. Now, a video has caught our attention in which a Bengaluru woman shared why she hated the city at first and what changed over time. Read on to know more!
Bengaluru Woman Reveals Why She Disliked The City At First

A post shared by Riyah (@laraeaffair)

title: Why A Bengaluru Woman Initially "Hated" The City, Now Can't Imagine Leaving It Behind | Curly Tales
text:
View this post on Instagram
A post shared by Riyah (@laraeaffair)
Instagram user Riyah (@laraeaffair) took to her handle to share how she felt when she first shifted to Bengaluru. She mentioned that at first she “hated” the city, and not because of traffic, littering, or anything like that, but because she had no friends, no sense of belonging, and nothing around her felt familiar. These challenges made it difficult for her to connect with the city in the beginning.
But recently, things have changed.
Bengaluru

title: Why A Bengaluru Woman Initially "Hated" The City, Now Can't Imagine Leaving It Behind | Curly Tales
text:
But recently, things have changed.
Bengaluru
has slowly grown on her. In the video, she said the city has become such an important part of her life that the thought of moving elsewhere no longer feels right. Interestingly, she revealed that she still does not have a large social circle in Bengaluru, and only has one or two friends there whom she rarely meets.

title: Bengaluru's Kempegowda International Airport Named The Best For Arrivals Globally, 3rd Year In A Row! | Curly Tales
text:
The airport's commitment to enhancing passenger convenience has once again placed it among the best in the world.
by
Jyotismita Maitra
Updated: March 20, 2025 12:28 PM
Share

title: Bengaluru's Kempegowda International Airport Named The Best For Arrivals Globally, 3rd Year In A Row! | Curly Tales
text:
by
Jyotismita Maitra
Updated: March 20, 2025 12:28 PM
Share
Bengaluru’s Kempegowda International Airport has been awarded the Best Airport for Arrivals in the World by the Airports Council International (ACI) for the third year in a row in 2024. This honour under the Airport Service Quality (ASQ) programme, which compares airports based on passenger experience and perception, is in recognition of the airport’s focus on making the passenger’s life easy.  The airport’s commitment to enhancing passenger convenience has once again placed it among the best in the world.

title: Bengaluru's Kempegowda International Airport Named The Best For Arrivals Globally, 3rd Year In A Row! | Curly Tales
text:
Why Kempegowda Airport Was Awarded Best Airport For Arrivals Worldwide
Bengaluru Airport is clearly compensating for the time it takes to reach it. Stunning.
pic.twitter.com/mCEP5XjP4n
— Gourav Mohanty | ⚔️PREORDER DANCE OF SHADOWS⚔️ (@MohantyGourav7)
March 16, 2025
Kempegowda Airport has earned international acclaim for its effective and passenger-friendly operations. One of the most important reasons for this success is its streamlined arrivals process, which allows rapid and comfortable passage through immigration and customs.

title: Bengaluru's Kempegowda International Airport Named The Best For Arrivals Globally, 3rd Year In A Row! | Curly Tales
text:
The customers have especially enjoyed the fast baggage delivery system, which saves time substantially. The airport is also equipped with a quick Wi-Fi facility, keeping the traveller connected right from landing. Also, hygiene and cleanliness have been given high importance, adding to the comfort of the travellers and providing a seamless arrival experience.
The ability of the airport to handle large numbers of passengers efficiently while maintaining the best service standards has been one of the main reasons for its continued success.
Also Read:

title: Bengaluru's Kempegowda International Airport Named The Best For Arrivals Globally, 3rd Year In A Row! | Curly Tales
text:

All IndiGo & SpiceJet Flights Operating From Delhi Airport’s T2 To Shift To T1 Soon; Here’s Why
Fascinating Facts About Bengaluru’s International Airport
Bengaluru wins the BEST AIRPORT for ARRIVALS award!
pic.twitter.com/dxWDcTYlMZ
— వై.ఎస్.కాంత్ (@yskanth)
March 18, 2025

title: Bengaluru's Kempegowda International Airport Named The Best For Arrivals Globally, 3rd Year In A Row! | Curly Tales
text:
pic.twitter.com/dxWDcTYlMZ
— వై.ఎస్.కాంత్ (@yskanth)
March 18, 2025
Ever since it opened in May 2008, it has been one of the prime factors in connecting Bengaluru. It has one of the most distinguishable aspects of being committed to sustainability and also being the first solar-powered airport in Karnataka. It has also gone green by endeavouring to construct a 50 km road using recycled plastic waste.

title: Bengaluru's Kempegowda International Airport Named The Best For Arrivals Globally, 3rd Year In A Row! | Curly Tales
text:
The Kempegowda Airport has been at the vanguard of gender equality, hosting India’s first all-women firefighting squad, a massive step towards the elimination of gender discrimination in the aviation industry.
The airport is marking India’s rich cultural heritage with more than 60 pieces of art by 43 Indian artists on display at Terminal 2. It also goes a step further in accessibility and inclusion, as it has a sensory room at Terminal 2 to assist neurodivergent passengers and sensory-sensitive people.

title: Bengaluru's Kempegowda International Airport Named The Best For Arrivals Globally, 3rd Year In A Row! | Curly Tales
text:
Additionally, it is India’s first airport to receive Level 1 accreditation under the ACI’s Accessibility Enhancement Programme, ensuring better facilities for passengers with disabilities.
Also, there exists ‘Tiger Wings’, a massive vertical garden where 630 species of native plants are found, hence representing a hotbed of biodiversity. These qualities position Kempegowda International Airport as a global best in innovation, passenger experience, and sustainability.

title: Bengaluru's Kempegowda International Airport Named The Best For Arrivals Globally, 3rd Year In A Row! | Curly Tales
text:
Kempegowda International Airport is more than an airport—it is a symbol of innovation, sustainability, and inclusion. As air travel evolves in the future, it is exciting to see how Bengaluru’s airport will grow and redefine the travel experience.

A pineapple is probably the last thing you’d expect to find in the luxury category. Yet for centuries, this spiky tropical fruit has been a status symbol, a scientific obsession, a royal gift, and in some cases, an item worth more than an international flight ticket! WHAT? Long before designer handbags and limited-edition sneakers became markers of exclusivity, Europe’s elite were showing off pineapples at dinner parties. Some never even ate them. As World Pineapple Day rolls around, here are eight extraordinary pineapples whose stories are every bit as remarkable as their

text:
World’s Most Expensive Pineapples
1. The Heligan Pineapple That Takes Years To Grow
Image Courtesy: heligangardens/X
Hidden within the grounds of Cornwall’s Lost Gardens of Heligan is a growing method that belongs in a history book. The gardeners here still cultivate pineapples the way Victorians did more than two centuries ago. There are no modern heated greenhouses. Instead, long trenches are packed with horse manure and organic matter. As the material decomposes, it generates heat, creating an artificial tropical climate in the middle of England.

title: From ₹34,000 To ₹10 Lakh: 7 Most Expensive Pineapples Grown In The World | Curly Tales
text:
The process is astonishingly labour-intensive. A single pineapple can take up to three years to reach maturity. The result is not just a fruit but a living demonstration of horticultural history.
Cost:
Approximately ₹1 lakh per pineapple
2. Rubyglow, The Pineapple That Looks Like A Gemstone
Image Courtesy: melissasproduce/X
Most pineapples are green and gold, but Rubyglow comes in deep shades of crimson and burgundy, looking more like something displayed in a jewellery store.

title: From ₹34,000 To ₹10 Lakh: 7 Most Expensive Pineapples Grown In The World | Curly Tales
text:
Created by Fresh Del Monte after roughly 15 years of breeding and research, the variety combines a traditional pineapple with the rare red-skinned Morada. The dramatic exterior isn’t its only selling point. Inside, the flesh is exceptionally sweet, low in acidity and remarkably juicy.
Cost:
₹34,000 per pineapple
Also Read:  5 Most Expensive Mango Varieties In India And What Makes Them So Pricey
3. Pinkglow, The Internet’s Favourite Pineapple

title: From ₹34,000 To ₹10 Lakh: 7 Most Expensive Pineapples Grown In The World | Curly Tales
text:
3. Pinkglow, The Internet’s Favourite Pineapple
Before Rubyglow stole the spotlight, there was Pinkglow. Cut one open and the fruit reveals a blush-pink interior that almost looks photoshopped. The colour comes from years of selective breeding that altered how the fruit processes lycopene, the same pigment responsible for the red colour of tomatoes and watermelons.
Beyond the aesthetics, many buyers praised its sweeter taste and lower acidity compared to conventional pineapples.
Cost:
₹4,300 per pineapple at launch
4. Queen Victoria’s Favourite Tiny Pineapple
Image Courtesy: dourleak/Canva Pro

title: From ₹34,000 To ₹10 Lakh: 7 Most Expensive Pineapples Grown In The World | Curly Tales
text:
4. Queen Victoria’s Favourite Tiny Pineapple
Image Courtesy: dourleak/Canva Pro
Size isn’t everything in the pineapple world. The
Queen Victoria
pineapple, grown primarily in
Mauritius
and
Réunion Island , is proof of that.

title: From ₹34,000 To ₹10 Lakh: 7 Most Expensive Pineapples Grown In The World | Curly Tales
text:
Queen Victoria
pineapple, grown primarily in
Mauritius
and
Réunion Island , is proof of that.
Much smaller than supermarket varieties, this fruit holds an extraordinary amount of flavour into a compact model. Its flesh is intensely sweet, fragrant and floral, earning it the nickname “the champagne pineapple.” Chefs prize it because every bite seems concentrated. Its limited production and reputation among gourmet circles have made it one of the most sought-after premium pineapples on the market.
Cost:
₹1,200 per pineapple

title: From ₹34,000 To ₹10 Lakh: 7 Most Expensive Pineapples Grown In The World | Curly Tales
text:
Cost:
₹1,200 per pineapple
Also Read:  Pineapple, Step Aside! This Aloo Gobi Pizza Is The New Controversial Topping We Love
5. Victorian Exhibition Pineapples, Britain
Before
Birkin
bags, there were pineapples. During the 1700s and early 1800s, pineapples became Britain’s ultimate status symbol. The fruit was so difficult to cultivate that only the wealthiest families could afford it.

title: From ₹34,000 To ₹10 Lakh: 7 Most Expensive Pineapples Grown In The World | Curly Tales
text:
But wait, here’s the strangest part; many owners didn’t eat them. Instead, pineapples were displayed as centrepieces at lavish dinners. Guests admired them and hosts bragged about them. Some were even rented out to other aristocrats who wanted to appear wealthy for a night.
Historians estimate that a pineapple from this era would be worth thousands of pounds today, putting its modern equivalent value somewhere between ₹5 lakh and ₹10 lakh.
Cost:
₹5-10 lakh per pineapple
Also Read:  Why Are People On The Internet Boiling Pineapple Peels With Cinnamon? Health Coach Reveals The Reason!

title: From ₹34,000 To ₹10 Lakh: 7 Most Expensive Pineapples Grown In The World | Curly Tales
text:
6. Kona Sugarloaf Pineapple, Hawaii
Image Courtesy: jfahmy/X
Ask pineapple enthusiasts to name the best-tasting pineapple they’ve ever eaten and the Kona Sugarloaf comes up almost too quickly.
What makes it special is the eating experience. The flesh is creamy white, almost completely fibre-free and remarkably low in acidity. The sharp bite associated with many pineapples is largely absent.
The variety thrives in
Hawaii’s
volcanic soil, but production remains limited.
Cost:
₹2,500–₹3,000 per pineapple

title: From ₹34,000 To ₹10 Lakh: 7 Most Expensive Pineapples Grown In The World | Curly Tales
text:
Hawaii’s
volcanic soil, but production remains limited.
Cost:
₹2,500–₹3,000 per pineapple
Also Read:  Sicily, Hawaii, Or Thailand? Find Your Dream & The White Lotus Destination & See What It’ll Cost You!
7.
Sugarloaf Pineapple, West Africa
If sweetness had a competition, the Sugarloaf pineapple would probably enter with innate confidence.
Native to Benin and other parts of West Africa, this variety is famous for its pale flesh and honey-like flavour. Unlike many commercial pineapples, it contains very little acidity, creating a softer and almost dessert-like taste profile.

title: From ₹34,000 To ₹10 Lakh: 7 Most Expensive Pineapples Grown In The World | Curly Tales
text:
The fruit bruises easily and doesn’t always travel well over long distances. As a result, high-quality export-grade Sugarloaf pineapples are relatively uncommon outside Africa, making them more expensive than standard supermarket varieties.
Cost:
₹1,300 per pineapple
Also Read:  What Is Spain’s Viral Pineapple Dating Trend Increasing The Sale Of This Fruit?

title: From ₹34,000 To ₹10 Lakh: 7 Most Expensive Pineapples Grown In The World | Curly Tales
text:
Also Read:  What Is Spain’s Viral Pineapple Dating Trend Increasing The Sale Of This Fruit?
The strange thing about this list isn’t that these pineapples exist; it’s that they’ve existed for centuries. Whether it was an 18th-century duke showing off a pineapple he had no intention of eating or a modern collector paying ₹34,000 for a Rubyglow, the fruit has always occupied a weird corner of human culture where food, wealth and bragging rights collide.
So, how are you celebrating World Pineapple Day?

Emirates and Qantas A380 aircraft face new wing inspections after crack concerns emerge.
