Share

title: Emirates To Inspect A380 Fleet After Wing Crack Concerns Trigger Safety Checks | Curly Tales
text:
by
Deeplata Garde
Updated: June 24, 2026 5:29 PM
Share
Some of the world’s biggest passenger planes are heading in for extra checks after engineers found cracks in parts of the Airbus A380’s wing structure. The inspections affect 16 aircraft in total, and almost all of them belong to Emirates, while one aircraft from Qantas is also included.
Airbus A380 Wing Checks Ordered for Emirates and Qantas Aircraft

title: Emirates To Inspect A380 Fleet After Wing Crack Concerns Trigger Safety Checks | Curly Tales
text:
Airbus A380 Wing Checks Ordered for Emirates and Qantas Aircraft
For passengers, there is no immediate reason to worry. Regulators have not suggested that the wider A380 fleet is unsafe to fly. But they do want airlines and engineers to take a closer look before small problems become bigger ones. And in aviation, that’s usually how things work best.
Emirates Has The Largest Number Of Aircraft Affected
Out of the 16 aircraft named in the directive, 15 are operated by Emirates.

title: Emirates To Inspect A380 Fleet After Wing Crack Concerns Trigger Safety Checks | Curly Tales
text:
Out of the 16 aircraft named in the directive, 15 are operated by Emirates.
That isn’t entirely surprising when you consider the Dubai airline runs the world’s biggest A380 fleet, with more than 100 of the double-decker aircraft still flying passengers across the globe.
Five of the affected jets will need inspections before they can fly again, as mentioned in a report by The National.
The remaining aircraft have a little more time and must be checked within the next 25 flight cycles.

title: Emirates To Inspect A380 Fleet After Wing Crack Concerns Trigger Safety Checks | Curly Tales
text:
The remaining aircraft have a little more time and must be checked within the next 25 flight cycles.
Also, regulators have allowed empty positioning flights if aircraft need to move to maintenance facilities for inspections.
Also Read:  Emirates Named World’s Best Airline Again, Clinching Three Prestigious Travel Awards
Engineers Are Looking At A Specific Part Of The Wing
According to Arabian Business, the area under review sits deep inside the wing structure in sections known as the mid spars.

title: Emirates To Inspect A380 Fleet After Wing Crack Concerns Trigger Safety Checks | Curly Tales
text:
But these components help distribute loads across the wing during flight, take-off and landing. If cracks develop and remain unnoticed for long periods, they could eventually weaken parts of the structure.
Airbus has already prepared inspection procedures, and airlines have been told to report findings within seven days, whether problems are discovered or not.
Also Read:  22 Hours In The Air? World’s Longest Flight Is Closer Than Ever As Qantas Completes Key Test Flight
Qantas Says Flights Will Continue As Normal
Picture credit- Wikimedia Commons

title: Emirates To Inspect A380 Fleet After Wing Crack Concerns Trigger Safety Checks | Curly Tales
text:
Qantas Says Flights Will Continue As Normal
Picture credit- Wikimedia Commons
Only one Qantas aircraft is affected by the order, as per Aerospace Global News.
The airline says the aircraft was already in Germany for scheduled maintenance work, meaning there should be no disruption to passengers or flight schedules.
That said, the inspections will add more work for maintenance teams as many A380S move deeper into their operating lives.
Ageing Aircraft Bring New Challenges
The A380 first entered commercial service in 2007 and quickly became one of the most recognisable aircraft ever built.

title: Emirates To Inspect A380 Fleet After Wing Crack Concerns Trigger Safety Checks | Curly Tales
text:
It’s enormous, with nearly 80 metres from wingtip to wingtip and capable of carrying hundreds of passengers at a time.
Similar checks took place in previous years involving different parts of the wing. For many airlines, it’s simply part of operating aircraft that have now spent almost two decades in the sky.
Cover Image Courtesy: Emirates/X

title: Mumbai Weather: Heavy Rains Flood Roads; Worst-Hit Areas, Traffic Diversions & What To Know | Curly Tales
text:
by
Tanvi Shrivastava
Updated: June 24, 2026 2:31 PM
Share
Monsoon in Mumbai is finally here, and the city woke up to extreme rainfall on a Wednesday morning. With heavy rain expected to continue to fall across parts of the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) declared a red alert for Mumbai and Palghar at 4 am. Read on to know more.
Mumbai Weather Today: Waterlogging In Various Parts

title: Mumbai Weather: Heavy Rains Flood Roads; Worst-Hit Areas, Traffic Diversions & What To Know | Curly Tales
text:
Mumbai Weather Today: Waterlogging In Various Parts
As per a report by India Today, the IMD issued a red alert that is valid for three hours, warning of thunderstorms, lightning, intense to very heavy rainfall, and winds of 40-60 km/h. The alert level was lowered to an orange alert at 7 am for the next three hours, with mild to extreme spells of rain expected for Mumbai, Thane, Raigad, Palghar and Sindhudurg.

title: Mumbai Weather: Heavy Rains Flood Roads; Worst-Hit Areas, Traffic Diversions & What To Know | Curly Tales
text:
Not only this, other parts of Mumbai, like King’s Circle in the Matunga area, showed trucks and other vehicles wading through waterlogged roads. Viral visuals from early Wednesday morning showed waterlogging in the areas around Andheri, Dadar and Kurla.
The monsoon reached
Mumbai
on Tuesday, 13 days post its normal arrival date, providing relief from the extreme heat. As per the reports, the India Meteorological Department stated that the southwest monsoon has further moved into the rest of the parts of the central Arabian Sea and other parts of Maharashtra, including Mumbai.

title: Mumbai Weather: Heavy Rains Flood Roads; Worst-Hit Areas, Traffic Diversions & What To Know | Curly Tales
text:
Also Read:  Mumbai To Welcome Its 1st Ritz-Carlton Hotel In 2027 With 238 Rooms, Ocean-View Bar & More
Wall Collapsed Near Vikhroli In Mumbai
As per the reports, amid the heavy rainfall, a retaining wall close to a residential building also fell apart on Tuesday night in Mumbai’s Vikhroli West. The important Dadar station, where the Central and Western suburban railway lines meet, was also waterlogged as heavy rain flooded the city.

title: Mumbai Weather: Heavy Rains Flood Roads; Worst-Hit Areas, Traffic Diversions & What To Know | Curly Tales
text:
Additionally, the city also recorded over 300 mm of rainfall in the 24 hours between 8 am on Tuesday and 8 am on Wednesday, as reported by the BMC. The IMD refers to a rainfall above 204.4 mm in 24 hours as extremely heavy.
The
Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation
further stated that all major subways in the city remained open on Tuesday, and traffic movement was normal. However, traffic was reported on several major roads due to overnight rainfall and flooding. Not only this, but the Andheri subway was also shut on Wednesday.

With the monsoon season in full swing, many travellers are choosing mountain escapes over city breaks. New travel data reveals a growing interest in hill destinations, including some lesser-known locations.
by
Priyanshi Shah
Updated: June 22, 2026 6:40 PM
Share

title: Monsoon Travel: Shimla Sees 76% Rise In Bookings; Indians Are Also Visiting Hidden Fems Like Dobhi & Rajgundha | Curly Tales
text:
by
Priyanshi Shah
Updated: June 22, 2026 6:40 PM
Share
Monsoon is finally here, giving us much-needed relief from the summer heat. But with it comes traffic jams, waterlogged roads, and daily disruptions that can affect travel plans. Zostel recently shared data revealing how travellers are swapping city trips for mountain getaways. Read on to find out how people are planning their monsoon travel this year.
Where Are Travellers Taking Trips To In Monsoon?

title: Monsoon Travel: Shimla Sees 76% Rise In Bookings; Indians Are Also Visiting Hidden Fems Like Dobhi & Rajgundha | Curly Tales
text:
Where Are Travellers Taking Trips To In Monsoon?
Zostel’s latest data shows that more people are choosing the mountains this monsoon. Bookings at Zostel Homes in Shimla jumped by 76% compared to last June. Popular destinations like
Srinagar , Tirthan Valley, and
Gangtok
saw almost 95% more visitors. We can say that mountain bookings across Zostel properties increased by 7-9%.

title: Monsoon Travel: Shimla Sees 76% Rise In Bookings; Indians Are Also Visiting Hidden Fems Like Dobhi & Rajgundha | Curly Tales
text:
These plans were not made months ago. Interestingly, around 48% of mountain bookings were made just three days before the travel date. Most of the remaining travellers booked their trips only 7 to 10 days in advance. This shows that people are no longer planning their holidays months ahead.
Also Read:  What Is Anti-Itinerary Travel? Why More Travellers Are Ditching Planned Trips For This?
Which Lesser-Known Destinations Are Travellers Exploring?
For a mountain trip, everyone’s first choice has always been
Shimla

title: Monsoon Travel: Shimla Sees 76% Rise In Bookings; Indians Are Also Visiting Hidden Fems Like Dobhi & Rajgundha | Curly Tales
text:
For a mountain trip, everyone’s first choice has always been
Shimla
with 76% bookings. But this year, people are also exploring lesser-known places like Dobhi, Rajgundha, and Kareri. These places attracted visitors through social media buzz. Meanwhile, places like Srinagar, Tirthan Valley, and Gangtok are attracting visitors from cities such as
Bengaluru
and Mumbai.

title: Monsoon Travel: Shimla Sees 76% Rise In Bookings; Indians Are Also Visiting Hidden Fems Like Dobhi & Rajgundha | Curly Tales
text:
Bengaluru
and Mumbai.
The monsoon travel trend is not only ruled by metro cities. In June, travellers from non-metro areas made up 67% of the footfall at Zostel properties, compared to 65% last year. While Bengaluru, Mumbai, Pune, Delhi NCR, and Hyderabad remained the top cities to plan a trip, people from Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities are also planning mountain getaways.

What Is Walking Pedestrian System In Ahmedabad? Check How It Helps Safe Road Crossing
Ahmedabad has introduced a trial-based pedestrian signal system near busy areas like CG Road to improve road safety. The initiative aims to help pedestrians cross roads more comfortably and in a more organised way.
by

title: What Is Walking Pedestrian System In Ahmedabad? Check How It Helps Safe Road Crossing | Curly Tales
text:
by
Priyanshi Shah
Updated: June 17, 2026 4:45 PM
Share
Ahmedabad has been growing, and we’re not only talking about large developments but also about small changes that can make a big difference in traffic management and road safety. Crossing roads can be difficult when there is continuous traffic flow on busy streets. To address this, officials have introduced walking pedestrian signals on a trial basis. Here’s how it works!
Ahmedabad Introduces Trial-Based Pedestrian Signals
View this post on Instagram
A post shared by Ahmedabad Updates | Food | Real Estate | RJ Manmeet (@rjmanmeet)

title: What Is Walking Pedestrian System In Ahmedabad? Check How It Helps Safe Road Crossing | Curly Tales
text:
A post shared by Ahmedabad Updates | Food | Real Estate | RJ Manmeet (@rjmanmeet)
On social media, RJ Manmeet (@rjmanmeet) shared a video of pedestrian signals installed in
Ahmedabad . If you are in the city, you might have too noticed them near CG Road. This idea has been introduced to ensure people can cross the road safely and comfortably. In Ahmedabad, people often simply raise their hand and cross or run, sometimes coming very close to accidents.

title: What Is Walking Pedestrian System In Ahmedabad? Check How It Helps Safe Road Crossing | Curly Tales
text:
Now, with these pedestrian signals, people who want to cross the road can press a button and wait for the signal to turn red. Once the signal turns red, officials expect motorists to stop and allow pedestrians to cross. After the set time period is completed, the signal will automatically turn green. The RJ Manmeet even demonstrated the process on the road and explained how pedestrians should use the button and wait patiently for their turn.

How Amdavadis Are Using This Signal?

title: What Is Walking Pedestrian System In Ahmedabad? Check How It Helps Safe Road Crossing | Curly Tales
text:
How Amdavadis Are Using This Signal?
Some people have started using these pedestrian signals. But here’s a twist. Even when the signal turns red, many motorists passing by are still not stopping. This initiative is a really good step for road safety, but it will take some time for people to fully understand and adapt to the new system.
We are now excited to see how officials will further improve awareness and ensure compliance, and how this initiative will change the way people safely cross busy roads in Ahmedabad.

by

title: After Mumbai-Ahmedabad, India Plans 7 New Bullet Train Corridors Connecting THESE Cities | Curly Tales
text:
by
Tanvi Shrivastava
Updated: June 17, 2026 2:38 AM
Share
India’s bullet train aspirations are set to go far beyond its first high-speed rail project currently under execution. While work on the Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train corridor is progressing slowly, the government has already recognised seven additional routes connecting major cities such as Delhi, Varanasi, Pune, Hyderabad, Bengaluru and Chennai as part of its long-term high-speed rail vision. Read on to know more about it.
India’s 7 Additional Bullet Train Routes
As per a report by News18, presently, the
Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train

title: After Mumbai-Ahmedabad, India Plans 7 New Bullet Train Corridors Connecting THESE Cities | Curly Tales
text:
As per a report by News18, presently, the
Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train
is India’s only active high-speed rail project. Spanning 508 km, the route is being built using Japan’s renowned Shinkansen technology and is created to operate at speeds of up to 320 kmph. According to Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw, the first stretch between Surat and Bilimora in Gujarat is expected to become operational by August 2027.

title: After Mumbai-Ahmedabad, India Plans 7 New Bullet Train Corridors Connecting THESE Cities | Curly Tales
text:
In another first for India, the project has introduced tunnel hood technology along the route. These structures are being installed at the entry and exit points of eight mountain tunnels, seven in Maharashtra and one in Gujarat, bringing the country in line with international bullet train safety standards.
Also Read:  Hyderabad To Chennai In Just 3 Hours? Can The Proposed Bullet Train Replace Air Travel Between The 2 Cities?

title: After Mumbai-Ahmedabad, India Plans 7 New Bullet Train Corridors Connecting THESE Cities | Curly Tales
text: Tunnel hoods play a crucial role in ensuring passenger comfort and structural safety. When bullet trains travel at speeds exceeding 300 kmph, they create sudden air pressure changes while entering and exiting tunnels. The hoods help regulate this pressure, preventing sonic boom-like effects that can cause discomfort to passengers and stress on the infrastructure.

title: After Mumbai-Ahmedabad, India Plans 7 New Bullet Train Corridors Connecting THESE Cities | Curly Tales
text:
Following the launch of the Surat-Bilimora section, the corridor will expand in phases with Vapi-Surat, Vapi-Ahmedabad and eventually the first inter-state stretch between Thane and Ahmedabad. The complete route will later connect Maharashtra and Gujarat through India’s fastest rail service.
What Are The 7 New Bullet Train Corridors?
Meanwhile, the government has proposed seven new
bullet train corridors:
Mumbai-Pune, Pune-Hyderabad, Hyderabad-Bengaluru,

title: After Mumbai-Ahmedabad, India Plans 7 New Bullet Train Corridors Connecting THESE Cities | Curly Tales
text:
bullet train corridors:
Mumbai-Pune, Pune-Hyderabad, Hyderabad-Bengaluru,
Hyderabad-Chennai , Chennai-Bengaluru, Delhi-Varanasi and Varanasi-Siliguri. These routes aim to connect some of India’s biggest economic, industrial, technology and tourism hubs.
Although no construction timelines have been announced yet, each corridor will undergo feasibility studies, approvals, funding arrangements and land acquisition before work begins.
Also Read:  Delhi-Varanasi Bullet Train Coming Soon? Travel Time May Drop Under 4 Hours, Check All Details

title: After Mumbai-Ahmedabad, India Plans 7 New Bullet Train Corridors Connecting THESE Cities | Curly Tales
text:
Once fully operational, the Mumbai-Ahmedabad corridor will serve as the backbone of India’s future high-speed rail network, potentially paving the way for a countrywide bullet train system that could transform intercity travel for decades to come.

They say, with grit and determination, you can reach the stars and this eight-year-old girl from Nashik proves that this adage could not be more true. The young girl Toshika Patil created a record by trekking seven forts in one day within 15 hours 30 minutes. She started climbing from Tikona fort in Maval at 5.30 am followed by Tung, Korigad, Lohegad, Visapur, Manaranjan, and Shrivardhan. We are in awe of her record-breaking feat.

The young girl completed this landmark milestone on March 1. She trekked Kalsubai with her family before undertaking the feat. Looking at her stamina and enthusiasm during the family trek, her family motivated her to climb all seven treks. As reported by Hindustan Times, the young girl enjoyed herself thoroughly while climbing all forts. She took some rest after climbing three forts in the afternoon after which she completed her milestone.

title: This Hidden Gem In Gujarat Will Transport You To Europe With Its Colourful Bohra Havelis & Old-World Architecture | Curly Tales
text:
This centuries-old town was once a sacred Hindu pilgrimage site and home to the magnificent Rudra Mahalaya Temple. Today, it stuns visitors with its pastel-colored Dawoodi Bohra mansions.
by
Mallika Khurana
Updated: October 11, 2025 2:02 AM
Share

title: This Hidden Gem In Gujarat Will Transport You To Europe With Its Colourful Bohra Havelis & Old-World Architecture | Curly Tales
text:
by
Mallika Khurana
Updated: October 11, 2025 2:02 AM
Share
To see European-style architecture, you don’t have to go to Paris or Rome. In the town of Siddhpur, Gujarat, there is something truly unexpected: an entire neighbourhood that seems to have been brought straight from Europe! Often called Gujarat’s “Mini Europe,” this heritage-rich town surprises visitors with its pastel-coloured houses, ornate balconies, and colonial-era façades that look more at home on the streets of Venice or Lisbon than in western India.
Siddhpur, Gujarat’s Town Of Victorian Mansions

title: This Hidden Gem In Gujarat Will Transport You To Europe With Its Colourful Bohra Havelis & Old-World Architecture | Curly Tales
text:
Siddhpur, Gujarat’s Town Of Victorian Mansions
Long before Siddhpur became famous for its colourful mansions, it was known as Shristhal, a sacred town mentioned in ancient Vedic texts. Siddhpur also has deep religious importance in the Hindu tradition. It is believed to be the place where Lord Parshuram performed the last rites for his mother, making Siddhpur a sacred site known as ‘Matru Gaya,’ the place to offer prayers for maternal ancestors.

title: This Hidden Gem In Gujarat Will Transport You To Europe With Its Colourful Bohra Havelis & Old-World Architecture | Curly Tales
text: But the most surprising part of Siddhpur’s story begins in the late 19th century. Around this time, the Dawoodi Bohra community, a prosperous Muslim trading group, lived here. The community was renowned for its keen business sense and global connections. They conducted business not only in Africa and the Middle East but also in distant places like Madagascar and Yemen.

title: This Hidden Gem In Gujarat Will Transport You To Europe With Its Colourful Bohra Havelis & Old-World Architecture | Curly Tales
text:
When they moved back to Siddhpur, these wealthy merchants wanted to bring back a little of the world they had seen abroad. Inspired by European cities, they began building magnificent Victorian-style mansions in Vohrawad (or Najampura).
The result was this dreamy little town that looks straight out of Europe’s 19th-century postcards. Walk through the lanes of Vohrawad, and you’ll find rows of pastel-coloured houses painted in shades of lilac, mint green, peach, sky blue, and rose pink. Each mansion has wood-carved balconies, tall windows with arches, gabled roofs, and decorative columns.

title: This Hidden Gem In Gujarat Will Transport You To Europe With Its Colourful Bohra Havelis & Old-World Architecture | Curly Tales
text:
When you look closely, you notice how the street planning also resembles old European towns, with neatly laid-out lanes, main roads connected to side streets, and symmetrical facades that make the entire area feel organised and artistic.
Also Read:
North VS South Goa: The Ultimate Guide To Picking Your Ideal Beach, Bite, And Experience
A Town Of Colourful Havelis And Centuries-Old Rituals
Image Courtesy: Wikimedia Commons/Nizil Shah

title: This Hidden Gem In Gujarat Will Transport You To Europe With Its Colourful Bohra Havelis & Old-World Architecture | Curly Tales
text:
A Town Of Colourful Havelis And Centuries-Old Rituals
Image Courtesy: Wikimedia Commons/Nizil Shah
The Bohra community eventually left their beautiful homes behind and moved abroad, as well as to bigger cities like Kolkata and Mumbai. And these mansions, with their pastel walls gradually fading under the Gujarati sun, now stand silent, their charm unaltered. If you pass through them today, it will feel like discovering a lost world.

title: This Hidden Gem In Gujarat Will Transport You To Europe With Its Colourful Bohra Havelis & Old-World Architecture | Curly Tales
text:
Siddhpur is a haven for people who enjoy architecture, heritage walks, and photography. The best way to experience its charm is simply to walk through its lanes. Spend a day or two soaking in the peace, the art, and the quiet beauty of a town that time almost forgot.
So, the next time you find yourself in Gujarat, take a detour from the usual and head to this “Mini Europe” in Gujarat, one of India’s best-kept secrets.
Cover Image Courtesy: Wikimedia Commons/Snehrashmi

title: 5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try | Curly Tales
text:
Home
/
5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try
Tired of the city buzz and looking for a breath of fresh air? These beginner-friendly treks near Mumbai are perfect weekend getaways. With stunning views, gentle trails, and historical charm—adventure has never been this easy.
by
Alifiya Burhanpurwala
Updated: May 29, 2025 9:31 PM
Share

title: 5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try | Curly Tales
text:
by
Alifiya Burhanpurwala
Updated: May 29, 2025 9:31 PM
Share
Many people assume that trekking is too tough to try, only suitable for truly adventurous people. But what if we suggest some beginner-friendly treks near Mumbai? For the first-timers/amateur trekkers, we have handpicked a few spots where you can go for an easy and refreshing trek. On the quiet hilltop, enjoy the cool breeze and the golden glow of sunrise as you climb up. These trekking adventures are the place to go this monsoon for an ideal getaway into nature.
5 Easy Beginner-Friendly Treks That You Can Embark Upon Near Mumbai:

title: 5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try | Curly Tales
text:
5 Easy Beginner-Friendly Treks That You Can Embark Upon Near Mumbai:
1. Harishchandragad Trek, Ahmednagar
Picture Credit: Wikimedia
This ancient fort was built in the 16th century, under the reign of the Kalchuri dynasty.
The fort will take you to the historical and cultural background of the bygone 16th century.
When you reach the top of the fort , there is a lake situated to the east of the temple named Saptateertha Pushkarni.
Khireshwar, Paachnai, and Belpada are the three villages from which you can plan your route to reach the fort.

title: 5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try | Curly Tales
text:
Use the Paachnai route for an easy trekking experience that can take your time up to 1.5- 2 hours (ascend and descend).
The ideal season to travel is during the months from December to June (pre-monsoon in general).
You can spot a huge Shiv Linga inside the cave of Kedareshwar.
Harishchandragad trek is a perfect trekking spot for a memorable trekking experience.
Where:
Start from the
Paachnai Village
Price:
₹699 to ₹1499
2. Peb Trek, Neral

title: 5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try | Curly Tales
text:
Where:
Start from the
Paachnai Village
Price:
₹699 to ₹1499
2. Peb Trek, Neral
For a weekend getaway near Mumbai, Matheran is at the top of the list for many tourists, making it crowded during peak travel season. So, this time, go for the Vitigad/Preb Trek, located close to Matheran, to avoid the bustling crowd. This spot was a watchtower for many ruling armies in history because of its 2,050 ft altitude. From the top, you can even gaze at the panoramic view of Matheran.

title: 5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try | Curly Tales
text:
So, this time you can ditch the same age-old Matheran trip and explore this unknown spot. At the top is a temple devoted to Swami Samartha, where locals even offer prayers. It’s only 80km from Mumbai and accessible by public transport.
Where:
Start from Anandwadi Village
Price:
₹890 onwards
3. One Tree Hill, Neral

title: 5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try | Curly Tales
text: Close to Karjat, One Tree Hill is perfect for an easy trekking journey with major attractions all around. Here, you can witness a scenic view of Morbe Dam and the serene natural beauty of greenery from the plateau. There is only one way to get there, and the walk could take three hours or more. Choose to start your trek from Ambewadi between June and September for the perfect experience. Enjoy your walk through the beautiful forest of Matheran, where you can sit beside the cascades, visible during the heavy rains. It is one of the spots that you should visit for a beginner-friendly trek

title: 5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try | Curly Tales
text: during the heavy rains. It is one of the spots that you should visit for a beginner-friendly trek experience.

title: 5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try | Curly Tales
text:
Where:
Start from Ambewadi Village
Price:
₹750 and ₹1299
Also Read:  Remember The Trek Bunny & Co Did In Yeh Jawaani Hai Deewani? Here’s All You Need To Know About It
4. Lohagad Fort, Lonavala

title: 5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try | Curly Tales
text:
4. Lohagad Fort, Lonavala
The trek to Lohagad Fort, built under the reign of the Lohtamia dynasty, is one of the shortest treks in Lonavala and convenient for beginners. Initially, this fort was tiring for the trekkers, as a proper tar road was not constructed back then to reach the base. But that’s not the case anymore. Unlike other trekking spots, the Lohagad Fort minimises the need to navigate the rocky paths. The paved stone steps help beginners to climb safely within 15-20 minutes. Here, you can spend your time clicking photos and diving into the natural beauty from the top.
Where:

title: 5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try | Curly Tales
text:
Where:
Start from Malawali Village
When:
9 AM- 6 PM
Price:
₹1,200 to ₹1,399
5. Yeor Hills, Thane
Picture Credit: Wikimedia
Yeor Hills is the easiest trekking spot for a beginner-friendly trek near Mumbai.
These hills not only offer you the amazing sights of nature but also have something more to enhance your adventurous experience.
With cycling, trekking, and jogging treks, this is the go-to spot for many newbies from the trekking world.
Just 10 km away from the Thane station, get a ‘Patlipada’ bus which will drop you at the last village of Yeor Hills.

title: 5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try | Curly Tales
text:
Or if you are coming from Mulund, take the BEST Bus number 110.
One can visit the Yeor Hills at any time of the month.
Also, to see the majestic waterfalls at Yeor Hills, you have to cross the river named Bhendi Nala to cherish the soothing and refreshing view of nature.
When:
Upvan Lake, Pokhran Road
When:
8 AM – 7 PM
Price:
₹58 (adults), ₹31 (kids)
Also Read:  5 Best Trekking Trails Near Dehradun

title: 5 Beginner-Friendly Treks In And Around Mumbai That You Should Try | Curly Tales
text:
When:
8 AM – 7 PM
Price:
₹58 (adults), ₹31 (kids)
Also Read:  5 Best Trekking Trails Near Dehradun
This monsoon, explore the scenic landscape around Mumbai from the top. Before you plan the trek, don’t forget to take the necessary precautions. Make sure to climb the terrain carefully, carry water and medications, and keep yourself updated about the weather. Once at your destination, take your time to feel the moist natural air that each one of us misses in the hectic urban city area.

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text:
India
/
Travel
/
From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas
Explore national parks, sanctuaries, rivers, lakes, and coastal waters where these endangered creatures thrive, You can enjoy boat rides, safaris, and guided tours that bring you up close to these dolphins.
by
Mallika Khurana
Updated: September 12, 2025 12:52 PM
Share

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text:
by
Mallika Khurana
Updated: September 12, 2025 12:52 PM
Share
Who doesn’t love dolphins? They’re playful, intelligent, and always bring a sense of joy the moment you spot them. While most people imagine dolphins leaping in the oceans, India is one of those rare countries where you can see both freshwater and marine species. What makes these sightings even more special is that many of these dolphins are endangered, so watching them in their natural homes feels like witnessing a secret treasure of nature.
Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins
1. Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary, Bihar

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text:
Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins
1. Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary, Bihar
Image Courtesy: Wikimedia Commons/Mr mrinal kaushik
If you’ve ever wanted to see India’s very own freshwater dolphin, the Gangetic dolphin, then this sanctuary in Bihar is the best place. Stretching 60 km along the

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text: Ganga River , this sanctuary was created in 1991 to protect these rare creatures. Locally, they are called Soons and are truly fascinating because they are blind. Yes, you read that right; these dolphins can’t see and instead use sound waves, or echolocation, to find their way and catch prey. When you take a boat ride here, spotting a dolphin suddenly jumping out of the water is magical. What makes the trip even more exciting is that you might also see gharials basking on the banks, playful otters, freshwater turtles, and lots of birds flying over the river.

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text:
2. Dibru-Saikhowa National Park, Assam
Tucked away in the northeastern state of Assam, Dibru-Saikhowa is a paradise for wildlife lovers. Imagine riding a small boat through calm waters surrounded by swamp forests and grasslands, and suddenly, a Gangetic dolphin surfaces nearby. That’s exactly what you can experience here. This national park is also known for something unusual: feral horses that are believed to be descendants of horses left behind during World War II. Dolphin sighting here feels especially unique, like a private moment between you and nature.

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text:
3. National Chambal Sanctuary, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh & Rajasthan
Image Courtesy: Canva/KeongDaGreat (rep image)

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text:
Image Courtesy: Canva/KeongDaGreat (rep image)
The Chambal River is one of India’s purest and most pristine rivers. This sanctuary, which spans three states, provides a safe haven for the Ganges river dolphin and the critically endangered gharial, a long-snouted crocodile that eats fish. The boat safaris are what make dolphin spotting here so thrilling. Dolphins frequently surface to breathe as you glide down the river, and gharials peacefully snooze on the banks. For those who enjoy wildlife but are looking for more than a fleeting sighting, it’s the ideal combination of adventure and tranquillity.

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text: 4. Chilika Lake, Odisha

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text: Chilika Lake is one of the most magical places in India. This 1,100-square-kilometre brackish water lagoon is the largest in Asia and the only location in India where you can see the endangered Irrawaddy dolphins. These dolphins are unique; they like swimming in schools, have round heads, and smile warmly. Sometimes, bottlenose dolphins may even sneak in from the Bay of Bengal! There’s more to this experience than dolphins. Imagine travelling across this enormous blue-green lake in a boat as fishermen cast their nets in the distance and hundreds of migratory birds soar overhead. The Irrawaddy

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text: cast their nets in the distance and hundreds of migratory birds soar overhead. The Irrawaddy dolphins offer the perfect finishing touch as they play close to your boat. This lake actually feels like a natural theatre.

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text:
Also Read:
From Royal Bengal Tiger To Saltwater Crocodile: 10 Animals To Spot At Sundarbans National Park
5. North Button Island National Park, Andaman & Nicobar Islands
Image Courtesy: Canva/ FourOaks (rep image)

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text:
Image Courtesy: Canva/ FourOaks (rep image)
The Andaman Islands are the best place to go if you want to see dolphins in a tropical paradise. Despite its small size, North Button Island National Park is surrounded by pristine waters that are extremely rich in marine life. Here, dolphins swim alongside dugongs (sea cows), blue whales, and vibrant coral reefs. While it is possible to see dolphins from a boat, you can also dive or snorkel to see them gliding gracefully underwater. With the turquoise seas, colourful fish, and forests everywhere, it feels like you’ve entered a nature documentary.

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text:
6. Sundarbans National Park, West Bengal
The Sundarbans are world-famous for their Bengal tigers, but the rivers and creeks here are just as exciting. The waters are home to both Irrawaddy dolphins and Gangetic dolphins, along with spinner dolphins and bottlenose dolphins. Some even say you can spot whales here! A boat ride through the maze of mangroves is an experience in itself. The trees rise dramatically from the water, and the silence is broken only by bird calls and the splash of your oar. Suddenly, a dolphin breaks the surface, and you realise how alive these waters are. The
Sundarbans

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text:
Sundarbans
give you the rare chance to see multiple species of dolphins in one place, all while being surrounded by one of the most unique ecosystems on Earth.
7. Dapoli, Maharashtra
Image Courtesy: Canva/Guillaume Meurice (rep image)

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text: On the Konkan coast, Dapoli is a hidden gem for dolphin spotting. At Murud Beach, local fishermen take you out in their boats early in the morning, when the sea is calm and quiet. The rule here is simple—stay silent, keep your eyes open, and wait. You will eventually see sleek fins moving through the water, sometimes in clusters of two or more. It is a serene and exhilarating experience. Just as you’re enjoying the refreshing breeze in the Arabian Sea, a dolphin unexpectedly jumps out of the water. The boatmen here are so experienced that they won’t return until you’ve seen at least one

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text: the water. The boatmen here are so experienced that they won’t return until you’ve seen at least one dolphin.

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text:
8. Goa
One of the greatest spots in India to see dolphins, Goa is more than just beaches and nightlife. Here, you can spot two kinds: the friendly Indo-Pacific humpback dolphins and the shy finless porpoises. The fun part is that dolphin tours are easily available everywhere, from Palolem in the south to Coco and Sinquerim in the north. You can even combine your dolphin spotting trip with snorkelling or
scuba diving
at Grand Island. Imagine sitting on a boat, sipping a cool beverage, and watching a school of dolphins swim and jump right next to you. It’s playful, lively, and perfectly Goa.

title: From Bihar To Goa, 8 Best Places In India To Spot Dolphins In Rivers, Lakes & Seas | Curly Tales
text:
Every dolphin-spotting experience in India is unique, from the blind Gangetic dolphins in Bihar to the Irrawaddy dolphins of Odisha’s Chilika Lake. They all serve as a reminder of how enchanted and full of surprises nature can be.
Cover Image Courtesy: Wikimedia Commons/Mike Prince

Craving Fried Food In Mumbai Rain? Skip Samosa & Pakoras For 7 Healthier Snacks
Skip fried monsoon snacks and try these crunchy, healthier alternatives during Mumbai's rainy season.
by
Deeplata Garde
Updated: June 24, 2026 6:45 PM
Share

title: Craving Fried Food In Mumbai Rain? Skip Samosa & Pakoras For 7 Healthier Snacks | Curly Tales
text:
by
Deeplata Garde
Updated: June 24, 2026 6:45 PM
Share
The Mumbai rains have a funny way of convincing people they need fried food. Suddenly, every shower makes you grab those crunchy samosas, pakoras and repeated cups of chai to fill a bucket. But if the weather has you reaching for something fried every other evening, it’s time to choose health and check out some healthy monsoon snacks.
This Monsoon, Skip The Deep Fried Snacks & Try These Crunchy Alternatives

title: Craving Fried Food In Mumbai Rain? Skip Samosa & Pakoras For 7 Healthier Snacks | Curly Tales
text:
This Monsoon, Skip The Deep Fried Snacks & Try These Crunchy Alternatives
Gone are the days when healthy snacks meant buying bland food for hefty prices. The market has improved massively. Some of them are genuinely crunchy, and some are surprisingly addictive. A few might even survive comparison with your favourite rainy-day snacks. So here’s a list of 7 healthy monsoon snacks to grab when it rains!
1. Baked Beetroot Chips

title: Craving Fried Food In Mumbai Rain? Skip Samosa & Pakoras For 7 Healthier Snacks | Curly Tales
text:
1. Baked Beetroot Chips
For everyone who made a disappointed face reading ‘beetroot’, trust me, this would change your mind. This healthy vegetable has a natural, sweet, earthy taste and has a crunch in terms of texture. Now imagine it when they are flavoured in your preferred masala and baked to crispy perfection. The deep purple colour is also an aesthetic choice apart from being tasty and healthy. Best paired with a yoghurt dip.
2. Masala Khakra

title: Craving Fried Food In Mumbai Rain? Skip Samosa & Pakoras For 7 Healthier Snacks | Curly Tales
text:
2. Masala Khakra
Easily available or, even better, to make one at home. It’s crunchy enough to satisfy cravings but light enough that you don’t find yourself fighting for a nap!
You get multiple masala versions like peri-peri or
pani puri
(yes, the market’s wild for this one). This choice is brilliant during monsoon evenings. Just break them into uneven pieces before eating, and we promise you will find your hand reaching out for one last piece.
Also Read:  Cardiologist Reveals 5 Surprisingly Healthy Street Food Snacks In India
3. Butter Popcorn
Pic credits: Canva

title: Craving Fried Food In Mumbai Rain? Skip Samosa & Pakoras For 7 Healthier Snacks | Curly Tales
text:
3. Butter Popcorn
Pic credits: Canva
Yes, we understand there are plenty of popcorn flavours in the market, but nothing beats the classic butter popcorn. It might be one of the easiest snack wins around. Don’t want it basic? Just add a little chilli powder, some chaat masala and maybe a squeeze of lime to completely change the game. Missing out on the handful-to-mouth ration is mandatory. Isn’t popcorn dangerously addictive?
4. Roasted Makhana

title: Craving Fried Food In Mumbai Rain? Skip Samosa & Pakoras For 7 Healthier Snacks | Curly Tales
text:
4. Roasted Makhana
For people who feel popcorn gets stuck in their teeth, Makhana is a better option. Roast a handful in ghee or add dried mint leaves to makhana, and you’ll have one of the country’s favourite healthy snacks. It’s crunchy, light and somehow disappears from the bowl much faster than expected.
5. Multigrain Nachos With Salsa
Healthy eating doesn’t mean saying goodbye to nachos forever. Multigrain nachos with guacamole or salsa still deliver the crunch people are after. Also, the avocado adds enough creaminess and good fats to make the whole thing feel indulgent.

title: Craving Fried Food In Mumbai Rain? Skip Samosa & Pakoras For 7 Healthier Snacks | Curly Tales
text:
6. Air-fried Falafel
Pic credits: Wikimedia Commons
Air-fried falafel bites come surprisingly close to satisfying pakora cravings. Crispy outside, soft in the middle and surprisingly satiating as well. Add mint, yoghurt, hummus or garlic dip, and suddenly the line between healthy snack and comfort food becomes very blurry.
Also Read:  Shikhar Dhawan Is Now The Brand Ambassador & Investor Of TagZ Foods, A Healthy Snacks Brand!
7. Lotus Stem Chips

title: Craving Fried Food In Mumbai Rain? Skip Samosa & Pakoras For 7 Healthier Snacks | Curly Tales
text:
7. Lotus Stem Chips
Lotus stem chips are still criminally underrated. Air-popped versions carry seasoning beautifully and bring a lighter crunch than potato chips. The first handful feels interesting because it’s nowhere close to your usual fried packet of chips. The second handful disappears without you noticing.
So if you find yourself reaching out for those oily snacks again now and then, here’re some perfectly healthier alternatives to monsoon snacks you can buy or even make at home easily. Which one would you pick first?

After Bengaluru, Michelin Bib Gourmand Thai Restaurant Lands In Hyderabad

by

title: After Bengaluru, Michelin Bib Gourmand Thai Restaurant Lands In Hyderabad | Curly Tales
text:
by
Ashmeet Guliani
Updated: June 24, 2026 3:41 PM
Share
Foodies in Hyderabad, rejoice! Bangkok’s celebrated Michelin Bib Gourmand restaurant, Baan Phadthai, has now landed in Hyderabad with its first outlet. The restaurant is known for its authentic street-style Thai cuisine, especially its signature Pad Thai made with a secret 18-ingredient sauce. So, if your taste buds are craving some Thai food, make sure you visit this restaurant in your city! Read on to know everything about it.
Michelin Bib Gourmand Restaurant Baan Phadthai Opens In Hyderabad
Image Courtesy: Supplied

title: After Bengaluru, Michelin Bib Gourmand Thai Restaurant Lands In Hyderabad | Curly Tales
text:
Michelin Bib Gourmand Restaurant Baan Phadthai Opens In Hyderabad
Image Courtesy: Supplied
Following its success in Bengaluru, Michelin Bib Gourmand restaurant
Baan Phadthai
is now in Hyderabad at Salarpuria Sattva Knowledge City, Hitech City. The restaurant was founded in Bangkok in 2016 by Frédéric Meyer and has been featured in the Michelin Guide
Bangkok ’s Bib Gourmand list every year since 2018.

title: After Bengaluru, Michelin Bib Gourmand Thai Restaurant Lands In Hyderabad | Curly Tales
text:
Bangkok ’s Bib Gourmand list every year since 2018.
The restaurant is known for its signature Phad Thai, which is prepared with premium rice noodles and an exclusive 18-ingredient sauce, apart from a curated menu that showcases regional Thai flavours. The 64-seater restaurant takes you to the backstreets of Bangkok. The interiors draw inspiration from the old shophouses of Bangkok and neighbourhood markets. The restaurant has deep blue walls punctuated by vivid orange accents, an aesthetic of the Baan Phadthai brand.

title: After Bengaluru, Michelin Bib Gourmand Thai Restaurant Lands In Hyderabad | Curly Tales
text:
Also Read:  Did Taylor Swift Feed Butter Chicken To Travis Kelce At London’s 2 Michelin Star Restaurant?
What’s On The Menu?
Foodies in Hyderabad now have a new destination to indulge in some amazing Thai food. The Baan Phadthai outlet in the city introduces eight dishes exclusive to Hyderabad. These include Yum Makuer (Thai eggplant salad), Gai Yang Vichianburi (Esaan-style char-grilled chicken), and a selection of new rice sets featuring classic Thai curries and stir-fries.

title: After Bengaluru, Michelin Bib Gourmand Thai Restaurant Lands In Hyderabad | Curly Tales
text: The starters include Gai Tawt (brined and fried chicken wings with homemade sriracha and tamarind naam jim), Poh Pia Tawt (crisp spring rolls with shiitake mushrooms and glass noodles) and Satay. Even vegetarians will have plenty of dishes to choose from on the menu.

title: After Bengaluru, Michelin Bib Gourmand Thai Restaurant Lands In Hyderabad | Curly Tales
text:
Complementing the food menu is a thoughtfully curated drinks list that brings Thai flavours to the glass. Diners can expect five inventive creations, each showcasing a distinct corner of Thai taste such as Jeed Jaad, Milo Dino, Som Tum, Cha Cha and Prik Gleua. There will also be Cha Yen (Thai tea with milk) and Cha Manao (Thai lime iced tea) for people who prefer timeless classics.

8 Vande Bharat Train Journeys So Scenic They Outshine Any Road Trip
We have listed eight Vande Bharat train journeys that are worth taking this monsoon, if you want to experience best scenic views.
by
Tanvi Shrivastava

title: 8 Vande Bharat Train Journeys So Scenic They Outshine Any Road Trip | Curly Tales
text:
by
Tanvi Shrivastava
Updated: June 23, 2026 6:26 PM
Share
As the monsoon season is almost here, we all enjoy long drives. And as dreamy as they sound, traffic snarls, slippery roads, landslides and poor road visibility can quickly ruin the experience. This is where Vande Bharat trains stand out. With large panoramic windows, faster travel times and comfortable seats, these scenic routes offer some of the best ways to experience India’s rain-soaked landscapes without worrying about driving conditions. Here are eight Vande Bharat journeys that are worth taking this monsoon.

title: 8 Vande Bharat Train Journeys So Scenic They Outshine Any Road Trip | Curly Tales
text:
Best Vande Bharat Journey During Monsoon Season
1. Mumbai-Goa Vande Bharat

title: 8 Vande Bharat Train Journeys So Scenic They Outshine Any Road Trip | Curly Tales
text: First and foremost, the Mumbai CSMT–Madgaon Vande Bharat is undoubtedly India’s most memorable monsoon train experience. The train that runs from Mumbai CSMT via Dadar, Thane, Panvel, Khed, Chiplun, Ratnagiri, Kankavali and Thivim before heading to Madgaon in Goa. On a journey through the Konkan Railway route, travellers are treated to countless waterfalls, vibrant green valleys, rivers, tunnels and scenic mountains, especially between Ratnagiri and Goa. Unlike the often-congested Mumbai-Goa highway, this journey offers breathtaking views of the Western Ghats from the comfort of your seat.

title: 8 Vande Bharat Train Journeys So Scenic They Outshine Any Road Trip | Curly Tales
text: highway, this journey offers breathtaking views of the Western Ghats from the comfort of your seat. Ticket prices start at approximately ₹1,600 for Chair Car.

title: 8 Vande Bharat Train Journeys So Scenic They Outshine Any Road Trip | Curly Tales
text:
2. Katra-Srinagar Vande Bharat
One of India’s most recent and best scenic stretches, this Vande Bharat runs from Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Katra to Srinagar via Sangaldan and Banihal. The journey travels through dozens of tunnels, snow-fed rivers, lush valleys and scenic marvels, including the Chenab Rail Bridge, which is also said to be the world’s highest railway arch bridge. During the monsoon, Kashmir’s scenery can become even more vibrant with cloud-covered mountains and green meadows. Ticket prices start at approximately ₹700.

title: 8 Vande Bharat Train Journeys So Scenic They Outshine Any Road Trip | Curly Tales
text:
Also Read:  Indian Railways Doubles Fine For Ticketless Travel; Here’s What Offenders Will Pay
3. Kasaragod-Thiruvananthapuram Vande Bharat
Image Courtesy: Wikimedia Commons

title: 8 Vande Bharat Train Journeys So Scenic They Outshine Any Road Trip | Curly Tales
text:
3. Kasaragod-Thiruvananthapuram Vande Bharat
Image Courtesy: Wikimedia Commons
Covering almost the entire stretch of Kerala, this train runs from Kasaragod via Kannur, Kozhikode, Shoranur, Thrissur, Ernakulam, Kottayam and Kollam before arriving at Thiruvananthapuram. The journey depicts Kerala at its monsoon best, with backwaters, rivers, coconut plantations, lush paddy fields, and the best part: The rain-soaked villages that are visible throughout the route. Ticket prices start at approximately ₹1,400.
4. Delhi-Dehradun Vande Bharat
The
New Delhi–Dehradun Vande Bharat

title: 8 Vande Bharat Train Journeys So Scenic They Outshine Any Road Trip | Curly Tales
text:
4. Delhi-Dehradun Vande Bharat
The
New Delhi–Dehradun Vande Bharat
runs through Ghaziabad, Meerut City, Muzaffarnagar, Saharanpur and Haridwar before it reaches Dehradun. The route offers beautiful views of sugarcane fields, rain-washed plains and the foothills of Uttarakhand. The final section after Haridwar becomes particularly picturesque during the monsoon. Ticket prices start at approximately ₹1,000.
5. Chennai-Coimbatore Vande Bharat

title: 8 Vande Bharat Train Journeys So Scenic They Outshine Any Road Trip | Curly Tales
text:
5. Chennai-Coimbatore Vande Bharat
Another best Vande Bharat journey is from Chennai Central through Katpadi, Jolarpettai, Salem, Erode and Tiruppur before arriving in Coimbatore. This route offers a stunning transition from urban skylines to green farmlands and eventually the Western Ghats. During the monsoon, the surroundings become greener, with massive clouds hanging over the mountains near Coimbatore. Ticket prices start at approximately ₹1,200.
Also Read:  Sonu Sood Applauds Vande Bharat Experience, Says Indian Railways Has Come A Long Way
6. Bengaluru-Mysuru Vande Bharat

title: 8 Vande Bharat Train Journeys So Scenic They Outshine Any Road Trip | Curly Tales
text:
6. Bengaluru-Mysuru Vande Bharat
Though short, this route from
Bengaluru to Mysuru
via Kengeri and Mandya is extremely rewarding during the rainy season. Mandya’s sugarcane fields, paddy farms and coconut groves transform into vibrant green carpets after the rains. The train completes the journey in under two hours, and tickets start at around ₹400.
7. Mumbai-Gandhinagar Vande Bharat
Image Courtesy: Wikimedia Commons

title: 8 Vande Bharat Train Journeys So Scenic They Outshine Any Road Trip | Curly Tales
text:
7. Mumbai-Gandhinagar Vande Bharat
Image Courtesy: Wikimedia Commons
This train travels from Mumbai Central via Borivali, Vapi, Surat, Vadodara and Ahmedabad before reaching Gandhinagar Capital. The journey becomes surprisingly scenic during the monsoon, especially between Vapi and Vadodara, where rivers overflow, and huge green fields stretch endlessly under cloudy skies. Ticket prices start at approximately ₹1,300.
8. Mangaluru-Madgaon Vande Bharat
The
Mangaluru-Madgaon Vande Bharat

title: 8 Vande Bharat Train Journeys So Scenic They Outshine Any Road Trip | Curly Tales
text:
8. Mangaluru-Madgaon Vande Bharat
The
Mangaluru-Madgaon Vande Bharat
travels via Udupi, Kundapura, Murdeshwar, Karwar and Thivim before proceeding to reach Goa. This scenic coastal route is one of India’s most overlooked train journeys, offering a rare combination of Arabian Sea views on one side and the misty Western Ghats on the other. During the monsoon, waterfalls emerge throughout the route, making it far more enjoyable than a road trip. Ticket prices start at approximately ₹1,300.

text:

Ambubachi Mela: 5 Lesser-Known Facts About Assam’s Menstruation & Fertility Festival
Held at the revered Kamakhya Temple, the annual Ambubachi Mela is a four-day spiritual festival that celebrates menstruation and fertility.
by
Tashika Tyagi
Updated: June 22, 2026 3:59 PM
Share

title: Ambubachi Mela: 5 Lesser-Known Facts About Assam's Menstruation & Fertility Festival | Curly Tales
text:
by
Tashika Tyagi
Updated: June 22, 2026 3:59 PM
Share
Ambubachi Mela, one of India’s largest and most significant religious gatherings, is set to begin at the revered Kamakhya Temple on June 22. As hundreds of thousands of devotees gather to celebrate this unique festival dedicated to menstruation, fertility, and feminine power, we have listed five lesser-known facts about the 4-day celebration that you should know.
5 Lesser-Known Facts About Ambubachi Mela
1. It Is Also Called The ‘Mahakumbh Of Tantrics’

title: Ambubachi Mela: 5 Lesser-Known Facts About Assam's Menstruation & Fertility Festival | Curly Tales
text:
5 Lesser-Known Facts About Ambubachi Mela
1. It Is Also Called The ‘Mahakumbh Of Tantrics’
The Ambubachi Mahayog, or Ambubachi Mela, is one of the largest gatherings of Tantric practitioners in the world, which is why it is often referred to as the Mahakumbh of Tantrics. Devotees believe that cosmic energies are at their peak around the temple during the festival. As a result, Tantric practitioners, Aghoris, and sadhaks from across the country gather at the
Kamakhya Temple
to take part in the mela.

title: Ambubachi Mela: 5 Lesser-Known Facts About Assam's Menstruation & Fertility Festival | Curly Tales
text:
Kamakhya Temple
to take part in the mela.
Also Read:   When Is Ambubachi Mela At Assam’s Kamakhya Temple? Why It Remains Closed For 3 Days?
2. Unique Prasad : Angodak & Angabastra
Every year around this time Ambubachi Mela happen in Guwahati, Assam and the doors of Kamakhya Temple remain closed for three days.
It is believed that during this time, Maa Kamakhya enters Rajaswala Avastha the sacred period of rest observed by the Devi herself.
A cloth is…
pic.twitter.com/D5FvNZRCdS
— Bhavesh Bhimanathani (@bhavesh_yuj)
June 23, 2025

title: Ambubachi Mela: 5 Lesser-Known Facts About Assam's Menstruation & Fertility Festival | Curly Tales
text:
A cloth is…
pic.twitter.com/D5FvNZRCdS
— Bhavesh Bhimanathani (@bhavesh_yuj)
June 23, 2025
Unlike the usual prasad offerings of sweets, flowers, or fruits, devotees receive two unique sacred items after the Kamakhya Temple reopens following Ambubachi Mela. These include Angodak, holy spring water believed to symbolise the goddess’s sacred fluid, and Angabastra or Rakta Bastra, a piece of red cloth said to have covered the Goddess’s yoni during her annual menstruation.
3. People Don’t Read Any Holy Books, Perform Puja Or Work

title: Ambubachi Mela: 5 Lesser-Known Facts About Assam's Menstruation & Fertility Festival | Curly Tales
text:
3. People Don’t Read Any Holy Books, Perform Puja Or Work
According to devotees, Maa Kamakhya is believed to be menstruating during this period, making it a time of sacred seclusion, rest, and purification. As a result, many refrain from reading holy scriptures, studying, performing pujas, cooking, farming, or engaging in physical labour. Just as the Earth is believed to be resting and renewing itself, devotees too observe this period as a time for pause and reflection.
Also Read:  Assam’s Kamakhya Temple To End Offline Special Darshan Bookings From June 15; How To Book Online Tickets?

title: Ambubachi Mela: 5 Lesser-Known Facts About Assam's Menstruation & Fertility Festival | Curly Tales
text:
4. Why The Brahmaputra River Turns Red During Ambubachi Mela?
It is believed that Maa Kamakhya menstruates during the four-day Ambubachi Mela, and many devotees associate this with the Brahmaputra River appearing reddish near the temple during the festival. However, many have pointed to a natural explanation.
Since the mela coincides with the onset of the monsoon, heavy rains are believed to wash large amounts of iron oxide and laterite-rich red soil from the surrounding hills and riverbanks into the river, giving the water its reddish hue.
5. Kamakhya Temple Is The Oldest Shakti Peetha

title: Ambubachi Mela: 5 Lesser-Known Facts About Assam's Menstruation & Fertility Festival | Curly Tales
text:
5. Kamakhya Temple Is The Oldest Shakti Peetha
The Kamakhya Temple, where the annual Ambubachi Mela takes place, is believed to be the oldest and most sacred of the primary Shakti Peethas. Located in Assam, it is believed to be the site where Devi Sati’s yoni (womb) fell during Lord Shiva’s Tandava dance of destruction.

FAQs
What is Ambubachi Mela?
Ambubachi Mela is an annual four-day festival held at the Kamakhya Temple in Assam. It celebrates the fertility, creative power, and annual menstruation of Goddess Kamakhya.
Why is Ambubachi Mela considered unique?

title: Ambubachi Mela: 5 Lesser-Known Facts About Assam's Menstruation & Fertility Festival | Curly Tales
text:
Why is Ambubachi Mela considered unique?
Unlike most religious festivals, Ambubachi Mela openly celebrates menstruation as a symbol of fertility, creation, and feminine power.
What does Ambubachi Mela symbolise?
The festival symbolises fertility, renewal, the cycle of life, and reverence for feminine power and nature's creative forces.

title: Allentown, Pa fire: Residents asked to leave homes as crews battle massive blaze on North Front Street | LIVE VIDEO - 6abc Philadelphia
text: Allentown, Pennsylvania . The blaze broke out around 8:45 p.m. in the 300 block of North Front Street. Chopper 6 video showed heavy flames engulfing a large commercial building as crews worked to bring the fire under control. The blaze has reached six alarms. Officials urged residents living on the 300 block of North Front Street to evacuate through the rear of the residence. Residents off Front Street but in the surrounding area are also being asked to shelter in place. No injuries have been reported. The cause of the fire remains under investigation. title: Music therapist at Shriners Children's Philadelphia hosts online show for patients and staff - 6abc Philadelphia
text: At Shriners Children's Philadelphia, their music therapist is making a difference in a creative way! Becky Dadi brings patients into a crafted vehicle to sing songs for a "carpool karaoke." "I'm using Carpool Karaoke Remixed as a music therapy tool to invite patients and staff to create a happy, collaborative, joyful and non-threatening environment for our patients," said Dadi. Current patients now have something to look forward to during their stay, and future kids can see that the hospital environment can be fun and welcoming. Becky's goal is to alleviate pressure, and allow children to feel

title: Lore's Chocolates creates the perfect souvenir for the World Cup and America 250 - 6abc Philadelphia
text:
Wednesday, June 24, 2026 10:23PM PHILADELPHIA, Pennsylvania (WPVI) --
For tourists and locals, you can find a Philly souvenir in the form of a sweet treat at Lore's Chocolates. They have seasonal items to celebrate America's 250th anniversary, and the World Cup games hosted in Philadelphia. "Lore's Chocolates has been here 60 years now. We manufacture our own chocolates, originally on site, now on Wyoming Avenue... and my father's 85 at the factory making chocolate every day," said Tony Walter, Co-Owner of Lore's Chocolates. title: Ukrainian refugee graduates from Philly high school with big dreams - 6abc Philadelphia
text:
Ukrainian refugee graduates from Philly high school with big dreams - 6abc Philadelphia
Breaking Crews battling massive fire in Allentown; residents asked to evacuate
Community Journalist Ukrainian refugee graduates from Philly high school with big dreams By Matteo Iadonisi
Tuesday, June 23, 2026 10:10PM PHILADELPHIA, Pennsylvania (WPVI) --
After fleeing from Ukraine during the war, Maria Steblyna became an accomplished student and an advocate for her homeland. Steblyna moved to Philadelphia and became a student at

title: Ukrainian refugee graduates from Philly high school with big dreams - 6abc Philadelphia
text:
String Theory School of Design. As a student, she led a student-led charitable initiative called, 'United Hearts United Nations.' Through the initiative, students like Steblyna joined forces with two organizations,
Bridges2030
and

title: Ukrainian refugee graduates from Philly high school with big dreams - 6abc Philadelphia
text: TEEEM . They held a bake sale, a charity concert, and collaborated with business leaders to raise more than $5,000. The funds, which will be matched by TEEEM, are intended to benefit Tomorrow by Together, a student-run nonprofit based in Ukraine. TEEEM, which stands for 'The Empathy Equality Entrepreneurship Mission,' works with over 130 schools nationwide. It connects students with global communities such as Ukraine. Bridges 2030 is a Philadelphia non-profit that helps forcibly displaced families and is particularly focused on aiding Ukraine. On June 12, 2026, Steblyna received her high

title: Ukrainian refugee graduates from Philly high school with big dreams - 6abc Philadelphia
text: families and is particularly focused on aiding Ukraine. On June 12, 2026, Steblyna received her high school diploma from Philadelphia Performing Arts Charter School, a String Theory Charter School, and an Arts Associate Degree from String Theory College's IDeATE program. The IDeATE program (Innovation, Design, Arts, Technology, and Entrepreneurship) engages students in critical forms of 'design thinking.' Steblyna aspires to attend Parsons School of Design. Although she was accepted, she is unsure of whether she has the funds to make it possible. Ultimately, she dreams of becoming a creative

title: Ukrainian refugee graduates from Philly high school with big dreams - 6abc Philadelphia
text: of whether she has the funds to make it possible. Ultimately, she dreams of becoming a creative director, designing events and fundraisers. And she hopes the war in Ukraine will end as soon as possible. RELATED: SEPTA employee surprises student with graduation card It was an act of kindness between a SEPTA station assistant and a Central High School student that made this graduation season one to remember. The

title: Museum in a truck drives hope across the nation, visits Philly area - 6abc Philadelphia
text:
Monday, June 22, 2026 12:05PM MANTUA, New Jersey (WPVI) --
The
'People of Hope Museum'
is an interactive exhibit in a truck that is driving stories of community service across the nation. The project was created by Catholic Charities USA in collaboration with various local branches, including South Jersey and Philadelphia. Inside the truck, guests can find interactive screens that display videos of community members describing impactful encounters with clients. One such storyteller is Peter Greenland, program supervisor of the Good Shepherd Program

title: Museum in a truck drives hope across the nation, visits Philly area - 6abc Philadelphia
text: & St. John's Hospice in Philadelphia. Also in the museum, guests can engage with a poverty simulator, discover data about poverty nationwide, explore a digital library of resources, and record their own hopeful video messages. The People of Hope Museum is due in Freehold, New Jersey, on June 22nd and 23rd, and in Philadelphia on June 25th and 26th. It recently appeared at the Church of the Incarnation in Mantua, New Jersey, which is the location featured in this news report. title: Lavender fields are blooming at South Jersey couple's farm - 6abc Philadelphia
text: in South Jersey were figuratively planted over 30 years ago by two high school classmates turned husband and wife. Kim and George Reeves met as high school students in Levittown, Pennsylvania. They got married in 1994 and subsequently moved to Glassboro, New Jersey. Then, they pursued their dream of owning land and managing their own farm. For most of the past 22 years, the Reeves family farm specialized in producing hay. But in the fall of 2020, they branched out into the field of lavender plants. Over the past six years, Field and Flock Lavender Farm has grown to over 2,000 plants. Guests

title: Lavender fields are blooming at South Jersey couple's farm - 6abc Philadelphia
text: Over the past six years, Field and Flock Lavender Farm has grown to over 2,000 plants. Guests who are interested in visiting the farm this summer have until early July before the plants are harvested. Field and Flock Lavender Farm is open to guests on Saturdays and Sundays. Visitors can stroll through two of their fields. Guests are not permitted to pick their own flowers, but can explore a shop full of lavender-related products. title: 'Salone FC' celebrates 25 years growing youth soccer in Philadelphia - 6abc Philadelphia
text: Foday Turay had a successful soccer career in West Africa. He ultimately moved to Philadelphia, where he's spent the last 25 years helping kids put their best foot forward. His Southwest Philly soccer club, 'Salone FC,' gets its name from his home country, 'Sierra Leone.' Since 2001, he and his community partners have coached hundreds of children. Some even hail from other parts of the world like Turay. This year, they are not only celebrating their 25th anniversary, but also the arrival of the World Cup in Philadelphia. Recently, some team members were able to view the FIFA World Cup trophy

title: 'Salone FC' celebrates 25 years growing youth soccer in Philadelphia - 6abc Philadelphia
text: World Cup in Philadelphia. Recently, some team members were able to view the FIFA World Cup trophy when it was on tour at the University of Pennsylvania's Palestra. Turay says the entire city worked hard to bring the World Cup home. "I was blessed enough to work with the board to put the bid in to host the World Cup here in Philadelphia," he said. title: Havertown woman honors her father in every step of 36 mile run - 6abc Philadelphia
text: Havertown's Caitlin Nelson was on a mission this morning. She ran 36 miles, for her 36th birthday, while raising $3,600 for Blood Cancer United. All of this was in memory of her father, who she shared a passion for running with. "His name is Nils Nelson...He had prostate cancer, which he was in remission for, but then...he had blood cancer. I was actually his bone marrow donor. So it was just kind of a full circle moment. We used to run together and definitely a passion and a love that we both shared," said Caitlin Nelson. "I think I'm just proud of myself...My dad was certainly a huge

title: Havertown woman honors her father in every step of 36 mile run - 6abc Philadelphia
text: both shared," said Caitlin Nelson. "I think I'm just proud of myself...My dad was certainly a huge advocate for running in my life and he was at many finish lines. It hit different that he wasn't physically there like I would have loved him to be...I always say...find people that during your hardest moments will be in your corner," she continued. title: After Injury, Bethlehem man volunteers for Special Olympics Pennsylvania - 6abc Philadelphia
text: Amy and Matt were newlyweds with excitement for their future before tragedy struck. "I was injured. I broke everything from my wrist to my shoulders...It's been a little over three and a half years at this point. I just had surgery 14 that was done, my wrists do not bend..." said Matt Reardon. Needing an outlet, he found Special Olympics Pennsylvania in the Greater Lehigh Valley Pocono Region. "I had nowhere to go. As time was passing, the depression kicked in...That's what got me in the Special Olympics... I don't have to use my hands now. It's using my mind. I can speak to people and

title: After Injury, Bethlehem man volunteers for Special Olympics Pennsylvania - 6abc Philadelphia
text: Special Olympics... I don't have to use my hands now. It's using my mind. I can speak to people and interact with people...And it's become more of a family than anything at this point," Reardon said. While becoming engrained in every piece of their programming, Matt discovered that helping their athletes was saving him in the process. "Our mission is to serve people with intellectual disabilities and make sure that they have opportunities, and we cannot do that without volunteers like Matt," said Julie Benjamin, Executive Director of Special Olympics Pennsylvania Greater Lehigh Valley Pocono
